**Whatever happened to New Year's Resolutions?** In the U.S., the _act_ of making New Year’s resolutions doesn’t look like it’s vanishing so much as settling into a steady minority habit: [roughly two-fifths of adults](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkr2OnDAQx5_GdKzM8LFQUKxy2VR3zSWKUiEvHhZfjL2yBwhNnj3C5pQoSpeG-Q3z8R-P_fW5exZKK3N_ERO2NNjO4Oo1EqHrJvFm3aoMofNk3WQ27IXpUXfAoUwzSKHupFB664pEtgMiYJ5gm52rqsmapqqTsa2bOuNNlpdF0Ze389DcMsDizIu8kbLKykS1e7cMoM4yXvHqVPTFrc6xH4aqr89SsoKjVGTd6c0qQyMOerMLulNvp0S3I9HDs_zC4Mrgutn5bpc9xOCKJp09g6twpHqNOxY1cEgNrumGwvnUobd6JmWNT4FDEQpyll9-svxC-INY_vR5RAYlcG3NfYcnUlP8Q05ItRcHzw7BTOK7ion8j-7BV9F4UloHetjHrIULvCoajwZmOwW6hK_BNdhvdv5kl4CvqIPYk0e3xFn87BbcdvwQ242qHw8RrVEGzEKccx68L6_BCDlr8r8rB2VkHJRGQTFFT9ZHpNXGs5gj-dBnUOfNTiWD5t_t-UOLWEX2fVWx-JDRKA4Va2Lg5f3sGLb0ERiU9T590_i_VhzHsXGZ-12eEtfKx6q0Vr46s4LfJ6F0eDb0X299aeFXAAAA__-J6_qq) say they make one in a given year. What’s changed more is the content—and the vibe—of the promises. In years gone by, resolutions read like a fairly classic self-improvement checklist: spend less/save more, exercise, lose weight, eat healthier, “be a better person.” Notably, “quit smoking” once showed up as a common aim. That made sense for that era: smoking was more prevalent, so “this is the year I quit” had a bigger pool of would-be quitters. Today, with cigarette smoking hovering near historic lows in polling, “quit smoking” naturally slips down the list. In newer polling, the menu expands. Health and money are still the backbone—diet, weight, fitness, budgeting, debt—but resolutions now more openly include mental health, productivity/organization, career goals, hobbies, travel, and being closer with friends and family. There’s also a subtle shift in _framing_ : more people talk about “goals” and “wellness,” not just quitting something. That lines up with research suggesting that approach-oriented goals (what you’ll do more of) tend to work better than pure avoidance goals (what you’ll stop doing). Either way, surveys show [only 13% of U.S. adults](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykU72O2zwQfBq6k0GtfiwVKozvPifNXZMLgqsMmlxJvKNIgaSsqMmzByKZIAHcpdEO9md2ZkF9e74-M6mkHl7YhJ3vzVXj6hR6j_Y6sXdjV6k9WueNnfSGnGmO6goUqiyHDJqrYFJt1_Iguh4RsDhgl5_qus3btm4OY3djoslLcav4qWha2rOqvUElGlqUJd7620F2O1sO0OQ5rWl9LHl5awrkfV_z5iQEKSkK6Y09vhup_Yi92swd7ZGb6aC60fvZkeJM4ELgspllMPe9ROCCOlscgQuzXnKFOywboJBpXLMNmXWZRWfU4qXRLgMKZRgoSHH-QYqzx--eFE-fzUqgAqqMHgIQJoQZzawwwN4olbr8aM0yjAEbHcIfSwhUxSUkYf8-FQFPRvvRBShj4GaawjQ9h8_rGBcZJWJdSZ5SYeK_ne5M_ch8SL5gVPOGzBKo_t_LDSVQta17oAkos5ENmduij2hyYh-xwLRI-V_uUscH4nwM6HVM6h1qLyfUUQtH65nUKvIyJYe0c5U-Mr2Z5ZO5P9TpFnvHONlLLaQekl6XLpxo83DJKib7EL5-iZ1iUT6RseRtTJSc6d8eAljmv5X5EaV9fDClQtzf0fFgOzGvUinp6hMp6TAxqcID9f_0V907-BkAAP__JUgcwA) say they can keep up with their resolutions for a full year. _Did you make any New Year's resolutions this year? The FDA has recalled millions of tubes. to see if you’re at risk and learn a simple household fix. | One final joke before the ornaments are boxed up, the leftovers are gone, and we officially say goodbye to Christmas for the year. | Whatever happened to New Year's Resolutions? In the U.S., the act of making New Year’s resolutions doesn’t look like it’s vanishing so much as settling into a steady minority habit: say they make one in a given year. What’s changed more is the content—and the vibe—of the promises. In years gone by, resolutions read like a fairly classic self-improvement checklist: spend less/save more, exercise, lose weight, eat healthier, “be a better person.” Notably, “quit smoking” once showed up as a common aim. That made sense for that era: smoking was more prevalent, so “this is the year I quit” had a bigger pool of would-be quitters. Today, with cigarette smoking hovering near historic lows in polling, “quit smoking” naturally slips down the list. In newer polling, the menu expands. Health and money are still the backbone—diet, weight, fitness, budgeting, debt—but resolutions now more openly include mental health, productivity/organization, career goals, hobbies, travel, and being closer with friends and family. There’s also a subtle shift in framing : more people talk about “goals” and “wellness,” not just quitting something. That lines up with research suggesting that approach-oriented goals (what you’ll do more of) tend to work better than pure avoidance goals (what you’ll stop doing). Either way, surveys show say they can keep up with their resolutions for a full year. Did you make any New Year's resolutions this year? name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Turn America back to Godly values
body:
Please - for the sake of all our families

| | | You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children.
---
| |
---
| | Dear Friend in Faith, Please - for the sake of all our families - take it from me as someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life... You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children. You may remember shows with wholesome messages like Father Knows Best, Marcus Welby, M.D., and even Dennis the Menace. But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on _Blue 's Clues_...
---
[](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkcGOmzAQhp_G3BwxY7DJgUOyhChSU1U5UPVkGXvIWgt2BIaqb19RdR9gb6Nf_4z0ffPzru_Gjz48v5uJart5PS86rXMwE83emt7YjxSf0Y1_NjNqzLHkgByrvQeZq62TAjOqQUl5RCigyN5rOTgahsqq_ghU9kYZLPPcYAUG1JCrzNf7JUCsAEQO4mArMUgQuaLjESwpVuTry5lEy8HGsNC8meQ38iHEfYrhYOOUjfV7Sq-FiRPDlmG7kF1nOphALqa9wbDtZ7MR72P8WBi2qKBEixV3JUle9EXBK5AVp7JXUpIaqiMw0a5p0ktcZ0tMNOfmyh-XjqHc44mcXycmmsv9f2LN9DL-GZhodqq8gFyfm6s-P07dRd-u119YftOPS6d_nPhbd_tciyFRSEw0b92Nf_rI5tq9fvtx9IvcLTwn48d_sOnr79lq_BsAAP__P36YLw)
---
| | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses. And the result is an upside-down world where children lecture YOU about what you can and cannot say... ...and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas. Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. **[Today, I'm inviting you to be a Founding Supporter of the very first children's television show that actively fights back against the groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical values that made America great.](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkcGOmzAQhp_G3BwxY7DJgUOyhChSU1U5UPVkGXvIWgt2BIaqb19RdR9gb6Nf_4z0ffPzru_Gjz48v5uJart5PS86rXMwE83emt7YjxSf0Y1_NjNqzLHkgByrvQeZq62TAjOqQUl5RCigyN5rOTgahsqq_ghU9kYZLPPcYAUG1JCrzNf7JUCsAEQO4mArMUgQuaLjESwpVuTry5lEy8HGsNC8meQ38iHEfYrhYOOUjfV7Sq-FiRPDlmG7kF1nOphALqa9wbDtZ7MR72P8WBi2qKBEixV3JUle9EXBK5AVp7JXUpIaqiMw0a5p0ktcZ0tMNOfmyh-XjqHc44mcXycmmsv9f2LN9DL-GZhodqq8gFyfm6s-P07dRd-u119YftOPS6d_nPhbd_tciyFRSEw0b92Nf_rI5tq9fvtx9IvcLTwn48d_sOnr79lq_BsAAP__P36YLw)** Introducing: _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_. I'm making this ground-breaking live-action television show with the great folks at **Brave Books**. If you've seen me and **Brave Books** on _Fox News_ , you know we've already countered those awful "Drag Queen Story Hours" the Left has been pushing on children in our taxpayer funded libraries. Thanks to support from parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens just like you, we started hosting Godly and patriotic "Story Hours" of our own. And now, THOUSANDS of children and parents can gather to pledge allegiance to our flag, read wholesome books, and pray together in their libraries. It's encouraging to see we can win when we fight back! Now we're working to have that same impact in television.
---
>> Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. >> Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. >> Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. Because we can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a **Founding Supporter**.
---
The great news is that we've already completed and released the first ten character-building episodes of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_! We premiered it recently to a small group of children and parents and I wish you could have seen the smiles on those innocent little faces. And I wish you could have experienced _the parents_ watching their children absorb the beautiful Gospel messages. It was quite a moment for everyone - including folks just like you - who worked so hard to get us that far. But now **Brave Books** has an urgent two-fold problem that I hope you can help with.
---
_**#1. Brave Books needs to launch a massive advertising blitz to get around the groomers on the Left.**_
---
You see, we've made the first five episodes of Season 1 of _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ available absolutely FREE. That's the best way we can reach MILLIONS of families with our Gospel-inspired messaging. But no one will see it if we can't get the word out. And the Left will try to bury _Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ at every turn. The price tag for an advertising blitz of this magnitude is $250,000 - and with the recent launch, we need to raise the funds immediately.
---
_**#2. We must complete Season 2 to keep our momentum going and that cost is $250,000 for post production.**_
---
I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously, we won't work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. But today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on Blue 's Clues... | | Or Sesame Street using YOUR tax-dollars to normalize same-sex parents and men in dresses. ## AI-Powered Medicare Pilot Program Coming Soon
Texas is one of six states that will [participate in a pilot program](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGupDAMQE8DXVBiSBiKFNvQzbZbIg8xg7WBoGDBzO1XzP5_gl9a1nvy85_7cEeOvD5_40JeXlMaVjr3SCKUh4UCj5hp45hky-mZcZGZFhpAg1UGFNyGgBzfQ1MG_9ChMyV50zrXQQ2uKWffOfMI9c1aMg9oLLUWcMTWaTOBbhyW7C-ZAbgZ0EbXVWOsq1F3NlhLMFHRaAosKVcyk9AL9ym-00G5GtNSRj-LbHtR_yqgL6A_z7P6-8L1WhbQXzUF9B_s_6iQ1XeY-opSklSmg-lUGwrTKmqnfPBIu-JVfWgV0igp72rGg9SY1pHyejnL7MN2coy8u7Zo9HNBjp_b5GcfPTz8CwAA__-Nm41P) that uses artificial intelligence to evaluate prior approvals for certain procedures for Medicare patients. Arizona, New Jersey, Ohio, Oklahoma, and Washington were also selected for participation in the six-year program, which starts Jan. 1. The program, dubbed WISeR (Wasteful and Inappropriate Service Reduction), [requires prior approval](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO2yAURp8Gb1hwsbEzMGSh09-MHa3bcG2jYoOAxMnbV07VJ_jne3R0v_Pra_pCH_y-_MSNTH3NcdrpKIFqpTxt5PwdMyUfYk05Lhm3utJGEwjouQQO4-TQh_fUNc78Fu4iGzJy0PoCCnTXrEahRNTOKSH1PKpRuB5AkRuc66AbdOPNKZMAowQhhWo72WuF4tK7vieYiXWCnK8xt3WlSi8sc3jHJ-X2HrcmmLXWVJi6MrAM7HEc7Vb288bA0s4fhYFdCUNdGdh_kwID-zFxX_jy8DshT37hfueVSuVx5ms8eMo-Zo4p5fjEUPjhQ-BHzH9O8H8cBhYzv17l7fbD7k02Lp2cL3pgnVg29OHzaP1e3qeBvwEAAP__JvSQcw) for more than a dozen medical procedures—including electrical nerve stimulation and epidural steroid injections—that "historically have had a higher risk of waste, fraud and abuse," according to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services. AI companies that administer the program will receive a [portion of the cost savings](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOozAQxvGngc7IHsCEwsU16XLtlWjAA4xiMLLnQvL2K1baJ9h-9NP8v3-P4YEceF_-4kZO3nMcdjpzIBFKw0aeJ0x0cIhypLgk3GSljQbQ0CoDCm6DRw6foSm9G7XvTUnOdNb2UINtytVhZ3Vzm-dOj9rWhmCeQXtjp7GZxnacSnYXZgBuBrTRddWY1tao-9a3LcFMRaPJs8RUyUpCb8xz-MQXpWqKWxncKnLkov5TwL2A-3me1ZVwEj2vgwLuPxnq5BBUFkyi_mfeF4WsVgqH2vBJarpMXEh5mjhz3LPa6S3qQ5gUGGN0X5fJ-eNiONuuaPSyIYfvP-R3670cfAUAAP__dI6GAg) associated with each denial, a model the American Hospital Association has characterized as a "perverse incentive." Human clinicians will review denials, and companies could be penalized if procedures they deny are subsequently appealed and approved. Massachusetts-based Cohere Health will [administer the program](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkT2O3DAMRk9jdxxY9N-4cLGNgRQbIEiR0uBKtE1ElgyJO965feBBcoLUH_EeHvjrfX4n8RLW77TzqF9LnAOf2bMqp3lnJ5YSH-KjHimuiXbdeOcZK2zBIOB9diT-OTelGz8qN5iSR9N33YA1dk25jUtPw2CQrXO9qRtqWmcHS33T9R_uTlzKeMEM4t1gZar61pi2q6kaWte2jAsXTcVONKabbqz8RXnxz_jgdLNxL_24qR65qN8KnAqczvO87TlcW4ETB_jMBU7KdgvRx_VZ4ERJZREr5EGCsveycrB8LfAvGf7mggR4OeEU7-EzM5CARqDjSPHBEBM4Dk-wnJQkQOb0EMsZNk4MGc6N9Lr_HeL5csPbm_kZvt1_lGl0x0WV3PVFU607iX816f994jHinwAAAP__21eh1A) in Texas. In a statement, the company said its AI-powered prior authorization platform approves 90% of requested procedures.
## Austin-San Antonio Megaregion Primed for Growth
Austin and San Antonio are 75 miles apart and have a combined population of 5.3 million, but the fast-growing cities are expected to [become a megaregion](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O2zAQBOCnkToa5EqkpIJFGneXNqWwJ67oBfgjkJvz-e0DB3mDtIMPg8H8-tg_kBOX-BMzefk-617o2ROJUNszBT6w0cWpytVqbJjlQZl20GCVAQXrHpDTa5_H4D912MxI3izObTCBm8eHX87NuHnTJ9BCuByBztUZa7fVgdUGR_bvMgOwGtBGT7fZWDeh3mywluCkYdYUWGq7yYOEvrGf6VW_qN2OmsfkHyJXH6YfA9wHuHcsWKQWro2u2uRWWxzgnrgLFYW_u3BRHYv6p1SmiI0i16K4q6NmLlGtalKZU3qnjToHKtLVJ8eD5dUzpiT1WQa4j82H6_mW3S3DrGNGTn-Hyf_d-eXhTwAAAP__6dmNgg) with more than 8.3 million people by 2050. Earlier this year, business leaders and government officials [met at Texas State University](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLvOnDAQRp_GdCB7wF62cJGGbtOmRLP2ACPZGJnZ29tHmzzC338XnfPnNt-QE-_rb8zk5b2UeafXmUiE6pwpcsBKB6ciRy1rxSwbZZpBg20NtDDOETl95qGJ_q7j1TTkzcW5K_Tghmbzfa9tGI0brAkGwYwY724MDgyMy510w_47ZgBGA9rovhuMdT3qq43WEiykBk2RpdRONhJ647mkT3lS7ULJTfKbyHGq_peCScGEj1N478IjyaNSxuObUjB9sRRMgeXTJl5IwXTi3uIuZefS_q-1mVZsM0ktCqam-ni8OCU-3UUNes3I6d-p_EzV08PfAAAA___f436B) in San Marcos for the first-ever Austin-San Antonio Megaregional Collaborative. At the summit, attendees [highlighted](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAUhJ8Gd1hwbCApKG7jLrfd0iLmYCPxY-GzcfL2q2QfYdvR6BvN93Wbby6mWNb_LqOlZ6hzwfNISIRtzujj4hruMVXaW12by7RhxhkEKC6Bw2X2LqbXPHbe3oW_yg6tNFpfYQA9dpsdDCxShaAc-tEsRqNUUppw0XcBRssu2jdMAlwkCCmGfpRKD05clVcKISAbBfpItfW0IeHTHSG96gNbv9TcJbsR7Qcb_jGYGEznefahPo37PiiWd4XB9P7EYPrN-OEKd4VqiZV_eDzj6njDNdbSNev3M6YUD23YKNbsYvos0d_8PCz8BAAA___Um3st) the strengths of each city and discussed challenges to continued growth, like affordable housing, job training, and water and transportation infrastructure. "_This area is going to grow_ ," [said Henry Cisneros](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUL2OtDAQexroQMlAslCk-Bq6_dor0SwZIFJ-UDK37L79idPpXuA6y7It2x_3-Y7Ou7j9x0CGX2uaI53FEzPlOZB1C2Y6nE985LRlDLxToBkEqEZCA8Ns0fn33NfWPIQdZU1G3rQeoQPd17vplodYSei1v1lEBaMeZKc6pYVetUBRO3OFSYBBgpCia3updIdiVFYpgpWqXpB1nHLLOzG9sKz-nZ6U2yWF2pud-ShV96-CqYLpPM82vAtGjJyiS5eogulaVcGEn4VdvEBmt3j6pZqCsfmxNIE2bDJtLsUGBOhBj0N77EedjT1O570r-lb1Ygvo_HcL_tt3TwNfAQAA__9KG4PU), the mayor of San Antonio during the 1980s and a former Housing and Urban Development secretary under President Bill Clinton. "_It's not a question of if. It's only a question of at what pace and if we're prepared for it_." Cisneros, the co-author of the [recently published](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OqzAQRp_GdCB7bP4KF7ehy223REM8gLU2RvYkJG-_yr7C9p--c3S-bvMNffDH9h8jWX6taT7oKoGYKc-RnL9jptOHxGdOW8bIO0WaQUJbK6hhmB368J5N5ewi3agqsqrvuhE0dKbaLUikHqRczChHNxitFyXXvu1AO41jX3n7OVMAgwKppG6MajuNcmxd2xKsJIwk5znlhndiemFZwzs9KTf3FKtgd-azCP1PwCRguq6rYYyPM1Mpn4WAaUnpW8A09oPqzEdBDyBg4p1qfBT2R8GjxoPT4VMdacNMm0-HgKnK1p2XD8GXrhdGbhF9-OXy32o9LfwEAAD__9yifSc) _The Austin–San Antonio Megaregion_ , emphasized the importance of regional coordination. "_Are we going to grow in a planned way with proper support, with some kind of rational transportation system, quality of life, opportunities for training, and a coherent approach?_ " [he asked](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuO6yAQhp_G7rDM4GtBcRp3Oe2W1gTGeCQwFmZDsk-_Sl5h6__ySd_Xbb0hez7cfwyk83OL60Hl8pQzpTWQZYOJTvYxnym6hCHvFGiFFnohQcC0WmT_Wrva6ntrZ1mTluMwzKBg6OpdT6O6b3KcezPjgMYomgeFaptNv1nEqWb9PpMAk4RWtqrpZD8obOfe9j3BRlXXkuUcU5N3yvTEa_Ov-KDUmBhqr_ecz6tS_ypYKlhKKY17pwcf7t2oYAno8IcPqmD57IXhzHQJOkz8TuhI4GGFiSeJwnkXAa-LHyRciiXvddL2LOw9X8NYda0LyP7Dzn8z9tDwGwAA____fINC). "_Or is this just going to be eclectic growth that creates traffic congestion, air pollution, and communities at each other’s throats? That’s the choice_."

Arizona, New Jersey, Ohio, Oklahoma, and Washington were also selected for participation in the six-year program, which starts Jan. 1. | | | | | | Sunday, December 28, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1859, the Hebrew Congregation of the City of Houston was chartered as the first Jewish house of worship in Texas. It was renamed in 1945. This is an abbreviated holiday edition of The Texas Flyover , which allows our staff to spend time with family and friends. We'll be back with a full edition tomorrow. If you’re ready to finally take control of your weight and your health, today's sponsor, , makes it simple to start—with trusted GLP-1 treatments prescribed online and delivered straight to your door. Take** The Flyoverwith you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | AI-Powered Medicare Pilot Program Coming Soon Texas is one of six states that will that uses artificial intelligence to evaluate prior approvals for certain procedures for Medicare patients. Arizona, New Jersey, Ohio, Oklahoma, and Washington were also selected for participation in the six-year program, which starts Jan. 1. The program, dubbed WISeR (Wasteful and Inappropriate Service Reduction), for more than a dozen medical procedures—including electrical nerve stimulation and epidural steroid injections—that "historically have had a higher risk of waste, fraud and abuse," according to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services. AI companies that administer the program will receive a associated with each denial, a model the American Hospital Association has characterized as a "perverse incentive." Human clinicians will review denials, and companies could be penalized if procedures they deny are subsequently appealed and approved. Massachusetts-based Cohere Health will in Texas. In a statement, the company said its AI-powered prior authorization platform approves 90% of requested procedures. Austin-San Antonio Megaregion Primed for Growth Austin and San Antonio are 75 miles apart and have a combined population of 5.3 million, but the fast-growing cities are expected to with more than 8.3 million people by 2050. Earlier this year, business leaders and government officials in San Marcos for the first-ever Austin-San Antonio Megaregional Collaborative. At the summit, attendees the strengths of each city and discussed challenges to continued growth, like affordable housing, job training, and water and transportation infrastructure. "This area is going to grow ," , the mayor of San Antonio during the 1980s and a former Housing and Urban Development secretary under President Bill Clinton. "It's not a question of if. It's only a question of at what pace and if we're prepared for it." Cisneros, the co-author of the The Austin–San Antonio Megaregion , emphasized the importance of regional coordination. "Are we going to grow in a planned way with proper support, with some kind of rational transportation system, quality of life, opportunities for training, and a coherent approach? " . "Or is this just going to be eclectic growth that creates traffic congestion, air pollution, and communities at each other’s throats? That’s the choice." | Flying together with our sponsor Ready to start your weight loss journey and take control of your health? With , it’s never been easier. Losing excess weight doesn’t just change how you look—it can lower your risk of diabetes, improve heart health, boost energy, and restore confidence. DirectMeds makes it simple and affordable to begin: complete a quick online consultation, get approved within 24 hours, and receive your medication shipped discreetly to your door. | ➤ Gov. Greg Abbott has called for legislation that would establish an office of Chief State Prosecutor, arguing that some local district attorneys have refused to prosecute certain criminals. "Progressive DAs are literally leading to the murder of Texans ," he posted on social media. () ➤ More than 150 Lake Austin property owners successfully petitioned to be removed from the city of Austin's jurisdiction, claiming they weren't receiving full services despite paying taxes. () ➤ NBC's Friday Night Lights TV series, which was filmed in and around Austin and aired from 2006 to 2011, is credited with revitalizing the Central Texas film industry and boosting the state's production credentials. Streaming service Peacock is currently working on a series reboot. () ➤ San Antonio's Texas Biomedical Research Institute is home to roughly 450 marmosets that are used for research on human aging and a variety of health conditions. The squirrel-sized monkeys, which "get chunky like we do," are big fans of mini marshmallows. () ➤ Abilene resident Reed Ambrose is the creator of , a new board game published by Czech Games Edition. "I've always loved creating ," he said. "I just love that I'm able to actually share it with people." () ➤ Austin-based Alpha School plans to open several new Texas Sports Academy schools across the state next year. The private school uses an AI-driven instructional platform that enables students to complete their coursework in two hours each day. And if what we've seen so far is any indication, we're in for a difficult fight. **What happened in Tennessee 's special election should be a wake-up call.** In a deep-red district, we barely held on. Why? Because too many of our voters didn't show up when President Trump wasn't on the ballot. We can't let that happen again. **WE 'RE 389 PATRIOTS SHORT of our urgent midnight goal. **If you stand for liberty, for our country, and for real conservative leadership, _you belong on our team._

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

[Watch the full video now](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=cc743ae4f14b40ecad3a574854c63010&_e=LcSVT69iehYG8zsdLZMaqbk--EVGahiiJd-0TM3j4FSUuzAGmuV3wdPIX7DfKy0qWwBwBwuqlCuWuuMQ2YXSSgkaQYk1H1glZkMsQudt99L6JrZRvHVWEm6IoYW1C0F2dqAXlVxYlDqn0vhhsgfln9F-6VF_kSlU13on2DwRqojn1VE46TRgGw-ATMAF0vj9jWVgumb9MLW8UhChDNbDWoaHr0z7tOuPnxcHEzEprY74m-qAYMqSCDszLrfjj0oyIV3zBSZn8aRLWExfQYW7V0y8UOoh6WTysv85ZgXhXt6Uf_NubBk6Av28al8z1V6Z5WYt7wyXZzBD1o9rMNl3Ujdz9liLB8XKfhbRQ6KP6TKsKcfVMRSeMFdR5yPpP-SgSiTOlGNXrTIsL8Azz-MkMq3Wd13v12PGDdFK0qSryrAZpVKtDGQzg6DijDVVf7_vOJGHY9johVf99Y9ntz6cx227fpEt1ZEPzNkv9-Qm7ywP-inQanojM7_BJVqOXZai0KyWODEYwtIx-h53mLnJQLH2yDmxI4X92dIzmcASXYEVIoJJqEK2IK06iO35p-b39WFswnEJV2BMObn1BjpCUg%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. This wasn’t a stunt or a launch demo. No crowd. No narration. Just the Cybertruck in motion — completely on its own. What happened during this unscripted moment caught everyone off guard. The cameras kept rolling. The crew stayed silent. And the vehicle did something that changed how we think about what’s next. People keep replaying the clip — not for the drama, but for the realization:It’s ready. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. clean_body: America’s financial system is about to change—the digital dollar could arrive faster than anyone expects.| | | | December 28 | | | | | Sudden Freeze: Imagine Your Accounts Locked Without Warning | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Team Wes Moore
subject: We can’t make any assumptions
body:
It’s always assumed that MD is a safe blue seat, history tells us otherwise.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1117%2E1086043%2Etb7PYs)
Peter,
Maryland is traditionally considered a “blue state.” _Here’s why we say otherwise:_
**Two of the last four governors of Maryland have been Republicans.** And just three years ago, we had a Republican governor.
Reelecting Wes is not guaranteed, with 2026 just _days away_ , we have to be prepared for a tough year ahead.
**Republicans are already jumping into the race.** We can’t take anything for granted. With 2026 around the corner, we need your support to keep Maryland blue.
**[So, Peter, what do you say? Will you show your support for Wes Moore with a contribution of $5 or any amount today before our end-of-year deadline?](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251228-1.rVyGlX&refcode=em-20251228-1&t=2&akid=1117%2E1086043%2Etb7PYs)**

Together,

body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40608%2E3422442%2EpcluMF)
Hey Peter,
I wanted to reach out today with a clear ask: we need to raise $75,000 by midnight on 12/31.
This isn’t about numbers on a spreadsheet for the sake of it; it’s about whether we have the resources to win.
Katie’s held big corporations accountable and fought for working people throughout her career. As Governor, she’ll bring that same no-nonsense approach to lower the cost of living.
**So here’s my question:**
**[Can you donate $5 or whatever you can today to help us raise $75,000 by midnight on 12/31?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20251228_1_ak.gMih6u&refcode=em20251228-40608&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40608%2E3422442%2EpcluMF)**

Thank you for being a part of this,
Thomas
Finance Director

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20251228-40608&amounts=&t=23&refcode2=40608_3422442_pcluMF&akid=40608%2E3422442%2EpcluMF)**

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=40608&t=25&akid=40608%2E3422442%2EpcluMF)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026

I hope this note finds you well. As 2025 comes to a close, I wanted to personally thank you for everything you've done to support the principles we all believe in: **limited government, fiscal responsibility, secure borders, and the rule of law.** Right now, **Chip Roy, Scott Perry, Andy Biggs, and the rest of the House Freedom Caucus** are the strongest voices in Congress standing against _runaway spending, open-border policies, and the weaponization of federal agencies._ _The Freedom Caucus Foundation_ —our 501(c)(3) non-profit—exists to research, train and educate the public on a mass scale to _advance the understandings of freedom and liberty._ **_It's just the beginning and we won't rest until all Americans understand the work Freedom Caucus members are doing to save our nation from a woke socialist nightmare._** **We're trying to close a __$127,000__ gap in our year-end budget so we can hit the ground running in January with voter education programs, research, and advocacy tools.** **A small group of year-end donors just stepped up with an emergency triple-match that expires tonight at midnight.** That means:

clean_body: | | | I hope this note finds you well. As 2025 comes to a close, I wanted to personally thank you for everything you've done to support the principles we all believe in: limited government, fiscal responsibility, secure borders, and the rule of law. Right now, Chip Roy, Scott Perry, Andy Biggs, and the rest of the House Freedom Caucus are the strongest voices in Congress standing against runaway spending, open-border policies, and the weaponization of federal agencies. The Freedom Caucus Foundation —our 501(c)(3) non-profit—exists to research, train and educate the public on a mass scale to advance the understandings of freedom and liberty. It's just the beginning and we won't rest until all Americans understand the work Freedom Caucus members are doing to save our nation from a woke socialist nightmare. We're trying to close a $127,000 gap in our year-end budget so we can hit the ground running in January with voter education programs, research, and advocacy tools. A small group of year-end donors just stepped up with an emergency triple-match that expires tonight at midnight. That means: | | And because we're a 501(c)(3), every gift is 100% tax-deductible for 2025. Freedom Caucus' mission running strong in 2026. Thank you again for everything you do. name: Chris Deluzio
subject: Republicans only care about their corporate bosses
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122108504/591611562/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNDhkODBiNi1mOWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wOOJq2IP_cZvQOA_kqGMi7F4n5g3XJKAWl3P2vDt5Ao=&emci=38934d28-77d7-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c48d80b6-f9e3-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=13185894) Derek,

I believe in something pretty straightforward about the American dream—if you work hard and play by the rules, you’ve got a shot at it.

**The thing is, folks are working hard—yet the cost of housing, groceries, and just about everything else remains too high. The corporate stranglehold on our economy is ripping people off across the country.**

Republicans have controlled all of our federal government for almost a year now, and they've done **nothing** to bring down costs. They’ve done **nothing** to break the corporate stranglehold on our economy, and they're not going to—because they do the bidding of their robber baron bosses. Here in Washington they care about one thing: helping robber barons plunder everybody else.

Their plan is tax giveaways to corporations and the ultra-rich. That is the only economic program they have. **But let’s be real, trickle-down economics has never worked for America. It never worked for Western Pennsylvania.**

That’s why I'm working to stop corporations from ripping people off—by breaking up monopolies and staying focused on what really matters: making life better for everyone, restoring the American dream, bringing down costs, and defending our freedom.

But here’s the truth: I can’t take on this power alone. The GOP is backed by robber barons and special interests that will spend whatever it takes to keep their grip on our economy. **[Every dollar you can donate helps build the campaign to stand up to them—one that puts working people first, not corporate profits. If that work sounds important to you, then please, donate what you can:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122108505/591611563/981825435?refcode=email_122825_RS2-020625&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNDhkODBiNi1mOWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wOOJq2IP_cZvQOA_kqGMi7F4n5g3XJKAWl3P2vDt5Ao=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122108506/591611564/981825435?refcode=email_122825_RS2-020625&express_lane=true&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNDhkODBiNi1mOWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wOOJq2IP_cZvQOA_kqGMi7F4n5g3XJKAWl3P2vDt5Ao=)

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: I need to say this:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
I need to take a moment to say thank you. It means the world to me knowing that grassroots supporters like you are in this fight with me. As Chair of the DSCC, I’m working every day to elect Democrats and build the nationwide strategy we need to flip the Senate. All the while, we’re confronting Trump’s agenda head-on — from GOP efforts to slash Medicaid and SNAP, to policies written for billionaires and corporate lobbyists instead of the people we were elected to serve. I’ll be honest, folks: With Trump in the White House and Republicans controlling Congress — it’s an uphill battle. But that’s exactly why we can’t lose our momentum now. In moments like this, our movement’s unity and hope are our greatest sources of strength. **That’s why our team set a goal to raise $7,500 from this email to keep our early investments on track — the voter outreach, organizing programs and candidate support that will help us take back the Senate.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122108716/591612073/-510661250?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.28_Gratitude_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZDhkODBiNi1mOWUzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LpBSqIVLFu46Bb67xs_p3jPkX-DqXsJ0YdbX-dJFEn4=) If everyone reading this chipped in just $10 (seriously, just a small donation!), we’d clear that goal in no time. | | Hi friends, December is drawing to a close. It’s been a whirlwind of a year. And before we enter a defining midterm election year, we wanted to share our New Year’s resolution list for 2026 with you.👇👇👇
---
| | ✅ **Strengthen Relationships:** Looking ahead, we need to keep this grassroots enthusiasm going by bringing more people into the fold, building connections with voters in every corner of CO-05. ✅**Take On New Challenges:** Show Jeff Crank and the Democratic establishment that Joe has what it takes to win this competitive race and flip CO-05 from red to blue. ✅**Long-term Prosperity and Health:** Lay the foundation for a winning campaign in 2026 and prove that we are energized, organized, and ready to take on Jeff Crank in the midterm. That starts with hitting every fundraising goal we set between now and the primary election.
---

clean_body: | | | | | Hi friends, December is drawing to a close. It’s been a whirlwind of a year. And before we enter a defining midterm election year, we wanted to share our New Year’s resolution list for 2026 with you.👇👇👇 | | ✅ Strengthen Relationships: Looking ahead, we need to keep this grassroots enthusiasm going by bringing more people into the fold, building connections with voters in every corner of CO-05. ✅Take On New Challenges: Show Jeff Crank and the Democratic establishment that Joe has what it takes to win this competitive race and flip CO-05 from red to blue. ✅Long-term Prosperity and Health: Lay the foundation for a winning campaign in 2026 and prove that we are energized, organized, and ready to take on Jeff Crank in the midterm. That starts with hitting every fundraising goal we set between now and the primary election. With your support, we can turn these New Year's resolutions into reality. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | ---|--- | | Thanks for your steadfast support. — Team Reagan | | | Joe Reagan is a Democrat, decorated combat Veteran, nonprofit leader, and devoted father running for Congress to flip Colorado’s 5th District blue. With over 20+ years of experience leading change and teams across the defense, technology, and nonprofit sectors, Joe Reagan is ready to step up and bring the concerns of our community to Congress. Support Joe’s campaign to help launch a new era of leadership in Washington D.C. »** | | FOLLOW JOE ONLINE: | | ---|--- Paid for by the Committee to Elect Joe Reagan

body:
The biggest and last goal of the year

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1749%2E49239%2EMo3kJT)

Null,

**[Is there anything I can say to convince you to chip in $5 before our critical end-of-quarter deadline at midnight on Dec. 31?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10959?t=3&akid=1749%2E49239%2EMo3kJT)**
What if I told you that **poll after** poll shows me leading the Democratic primary – and I have the best shot of any Democrat at defeating MAGA Mike Rogers in November?

What if I told you that **Michigan is now ranked one of the most likely Senate seats to flip** – and national Republicans are targeting our race?
What if I told you that **MAGA megadonors are already spending big****to flip Michigan red in 2026** – and they’re just getting started?

What if I told you that **I’m doing especially well with the all-important independent voters** – and one political expert called it “independent magic”?
**What if I told you that the final FEC deadline of the year is almost here – and****what we report will shape this race in a _BIG_ way as we head into 2026?**

_What if I told you we can win this race,_ but __only__ if this grassroots team steps up right now?
If I told you all that, null, **[would you chip in $5 now to keep Michigan blue and help us hit our critical $500,000 end-of-quarter goal?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10959?t=4&akid=1749%2E49239%2EMo3kJT)**

**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10909?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=9&akid=1749%2E49239%2EMo3kJT) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11060?t=10&akid=1749%2E49239%2EMo3kJT)

Haley

clean_body: Friends, The Democrats are suing to force an illegal redistricting scheme designed to protect their power and wipe out opposition. We did not start this fight. We are going to court to defend our district and the voters who live in it. | Friends, The Democrats are suing to force an illegal redistricting scheme designed to protect their power and wipe out opposition. We did not start this fight. We are going to court to defend our district and the voters who live in it. | | This case is about stopping politicians from rigging elections after they do not like the outcome. Defending our district in court is expensive, but surrendering it is not an option. | | | | | | | | | | | | | Thank you, Nicole | Nicole for New York, PO Box 60487, Staten Island, NY 10306

name: Chris Pappas
subject: Is there anything I can say?
body:
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1001&akid=4267%2E582294%2Esqnz_v ]Chris Pappas for Senate[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1002&akid=4267%2E582294%2Esqnz_v ]Chris Pappas for Senate

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251228_1.CR36vQ&refcode=em20251228-1&t=1003&akid=4267%2E582294%2Esqnz_v ]Is there anything I can say to convince you to chip in $5 or $10 before
our final FEC deadline on December 31st?

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251228_1.CR36vQ&refcode=em20251228-1&t=1004&akid=4267%2E582294%2Esqnz_v ]Chip In »

What if I told you that our chances of winning were downgraded after my
opponent, John Sununu, jumped in, and back-to-back polls show us in a dead
heat?

Or that Republicans see New Hampshire as a top pick-up opportunity and are
lining up behind my opponent to buy this open seat?

How about if I told you that Democrats have no shot at flipping the Senate
without winning here?

But here’s the thing. I’m battle-tested. I’ve
won four competitive elections for Congress thanks to a strong grassroots
team. I’ve built trust with voters from all corners who know that New
Hampshire always comes first for me.

This election will be our toughest fight yet, and I need that same
grassroots team to take action right now.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251228_1.CR36vQ&refcode=em20251228-1&t=1005&akid=4267%2E582294%2Esqnz_v ]With all eyes on this race, will you rush $5 or any
amount before our final FEC deadline of 2025? What you do right now will
set the course for the next year.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251228_1.CR36vQ&amount=5&refcode=em20251228-1&t=1024&akid=4267%2E582294%2Esqnz_v ]Donate $5 »

Thank you in advance,

Chris Pappas

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.chrispappas.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=4267%2E582294%2Esqnz_v

name: Scout Walz
subject: tim needs our help
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/195?refcode=em20251228-364&t=1&akid=364%2E34582%2ECkHO1M)
[](https://act.timwalz.com/go/219?t=2&akid=364%2E34582%2ECkHO1M)
hey there, it’s scout! 🐾
you might not think that a dog like me knows much about politics, but when you live in the walz house like me, you hear quite a bit about it. making life better for minnesotans is almost all my dad tim talks about.
between our morning runs and rides in the car, my dad’s been talking about something called an “end-of-year deadline.” apparently it’s a pretty big deal for his campaign (i think it means my dad gets a lot of treats)?
my dad is running against a lot of bad republicans. one of them even got trump’s support (grrr). my dad is stressed about reaching his fundraising goal before the deadline so he can beat any of them.
**[politics are important, even for dogs! i see my dad fight so hard for minnesota, and i just know he has to win. can you chip in $5 to his campaign? it will make him as happy as treats make me!](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251228_1.zKW9vB&amount=5&refcode=em20251228-364&t=3&akid=364%2E34582%2ECkHO1M)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251228_1.zKW9vB&amount=5&refcode=20251228-364&t=22&akid=364%2E34582%2ECkHO1M)**
thanks fur everything,
scout

clean_body: hey there, it’s scout! 🐾 you might not think that a dog like me knows much about politics, but when you live in the walz house like me, you hear quite a bit about it. making life better for minnesotans is almost all my dad tim talks about. between our morning runs and rides in the car, my dad’s been talking about something called an “end-of-year deadline.” apparently it’s a pretty big deal for his campaign (i think it means my dad gets a lot of treats)? my dad is running against a lot of bad republicans. one of them even got trump’s support (grrr). my dad is stressed about reaching his fundraising goal before the deadline so he can beat any of them. thanks fur everything, scout Email us: mailto:info@walzforgovernor.org

subject: Billionaire Money Never Rests

body:
They are betting that working people will be tired, distracted, and outspent | | | Billionaire Money Never Rests Peter, Billionaire money does not take holidays. It does not pause for the end of the year. It does not rest. With three days left in 2025, the same donors and lobbying networks that have warped our politics for decades are already pouring money into 2026. They are betting that working people will be tired, distracted, and outspent before this race even begins. That is why this matters now. Last month, I was named one of just 42 candidates for Congress endorsed by Citizens Against AIPAC Corruption. Right now, only 65 elected members of Congress refuse to accept corporate PAC money. If we want better working class representation in Congress, we need to close this year strong. Please consider making a contribution before the year ends. clean_body: They are betting that working people will be tired, distracted, and outspent | | | Billionaire Money Never Rests Peter, Billionaire money does not take holidays. It does not pause for the end of the year. It does not rest. With three days left in 2025, the same donors and lobbying networks that have warped our politics for decades are already pouring money into 2026. They are betting that working people will be tired, distracted, and outspent before this race even begins. That is why this matters now. Last month, I was named one of just 42 candidates for Congress endorsed by Citizens Against AIPAC Corruption. Right now, only 65 elected members of Congress refuse to accept corporate PAC money. If we want better working class representation in Congress, we need to close this year strong. Please consider making a contribution before the year ends. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Or mail a check to: P.O. body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71674?t=3&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY) [ **So, Robert Reich is calling on Congress to pass a Constitutional Amendment to abolish the Electoral College! We need 50,000 signatures from EVERY STATE before midnight to prove the public is behind him!**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71674?t=4&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY) | [ADD MY NAME TO THE PETITION →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71674?t=5&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY)
---
* * *
If you were thinking of ignoring our urgent petition, here’s why you SHOULDN’T:
[**→ Donald Trump only became President in 2016 because of the outdated Electoral College.

→→ Hillary Clinton won the popular vote by nearly 2.9 million.

→→→ But the Electoral College overruled her victory!! **](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71674?t=6&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY)
We can’t let something like that happen again! That’s why Robert Reich is SOUNDING THE ALARM:
[**“Twice in the last generation, the Electoral College has handed the presidency to someone who lost the popular vote.”** ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71674?t=7&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY) | [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71674?t=8&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY)

The Electoral College is a BROKEN system, and that’s exactly why we need you to sign today, team.
[ **If every Democrat reading this signs our petition, we will reach our 125,OOO signature goal within minutes and gain traction for this urgent legislation in Congress. Please add your name immediately:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71674?t=9&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY)
[ADD MY NAME TO THE PETITION →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71674?t=10&akid=23786%2E4449941%2ECXlrJY)

Trump is going to hate this!

name: Team Taylor
subject: Everyone will be watching
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
Our campaign is facing a major public fundraising deadline on December 31.
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251228-950&t=1&akid=961%2E294397%2EfSnKag)
We know it’s a Sunday, so we’ll keep this short — promise. 🙏
Our campaign is facing a major public fundraising deadline on December 31.
After the deadline, our campaign finances will be **publicly reported** for _everyone_ to see, including our extreme opponent, right-wing special interests, and the media.
Our report will shape how aggressively outside money moves against us in the months ahead. That’s why we must showcase this grassroots team’s momentum to ward off any attacks.
[**If you’re able to, will you chip in $10 before December 31 to help us report the strongest numbers possible? Any amount helps.**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251228_1_ak.Yv7Hx_&amount=10&refcode=em20251228-950&t=3&akid=961%2E294397%2EfSnKag)

Thank you,
Team Taylor

name: Team Cooper
subject: ⏳ We’re running out of time…
body:
Roy’s counting on you before the year is up.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=225%2E124072%2E9Aa0Z6)
Sorry to bother you on a Sunday, Peter, _but we’re almost out of time._ **This is URGENT: We’re closing the books on 2025 in 72 hours.** We _need_ to meet our $1,000,000 end-of-quarter fundraising goal before then if we want to send Roy Cooper to the U.S. Senate and take back the majority. **[Time is running out. Rush a donation ASAP to help meet our LAST end-of-quarter goal of 2025 and send Roy Cooper to the Senate>>>](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3272?t=7&akid=225%2E124072%2E9Aa0Z6)** **We’ve told you why this deadline matters so much:** Next month, our fundraising numbers go public. Roy's opponent, the GOP, and their dark money donors will analyze every inch of it and decide how much MORE money to dump into defeating us. _We can't give them an opening._ This Senate seat belongs to the people of North Carolina — _not special interests, not billionaires, not right-wing extremists._ That’s why we’re asking you to give Roy the resources he needs to beat Michael Whatley and his GOP mega-donors. **We can’t hit this goal without you.** **[We have 72 hours left. Chip in $5 — or any amount that works for you — right now to help Roy Cooper beat the GOP in North Carolina and flip the Senate blue in 2026>>>](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3272?amount=5&t=8&akid=225%2E124072%2E9Aa0Z6)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3273?t=9&akid=225%2E124072%2E9Aa0Z6)

name: Ilhan Omar for Congress
subject: ICYMI: Trump is investigating Ilhan.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
This is a major escalation. Help us fight back.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=2013%2E791427%2Eh6W9RK)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251228-1-nor&t=2&akid=2013%2E791427%2Eh6W9RK)
**Friend — This is a major escalation.**
Trump has been going after Ilhan for weeks, calling her “garbage”, insulting her religion, and spreading dangerous and racist conspiracy theories.
But now, Trump’s “Border Czar”, Tom Homan, has confirmed that **the Trump administration is**investigating Ilhan Omar** in an attempt to punish her with criminal charges.**
**[PLEASE, rush a donation to stand with Ilhan Omar and help us fight back against this sham investigation >>**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251228-1-nor&t=3&akid=2013%2E791427%2Eh6W9RK)
**[RUSH A DONATION](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251228-1-nor&t=4&akid=2013%2E791427%2Eh6W9RK)**
Friend — Make no mistake, this is a highly political investigation with no basis in reality.
This administration is getting their information from conspiracy theorists and amplifying long-debunked lies about Ilhan and her family.
But Trump’s goal isn’t to seek the truth. His goal is to use the power of the federal government to intimidate Ilhan into silence and scapegoat immigrants like her, just like what he is doing with his inhumane ICE raids and racial-profiling on the streets of Minneapolis.
Well, we’re not going to sit idly by while Trump makes a mockery of justice.
It’s our job to show the MAGA far-right that Ilhan has a huge grassroots movement behind her, and that if they want to come for her, they have to come for us, too.
**[Help us fight back against this sham investigation by chipping in to power our movement today. We need the help of everyone on this team to weather this storm and keep up the fight against Trump’s corrupt administration.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251228-1-nor&t=5&akid=2013%2E791427%2Eh6W9RK)

When you give to the USO right now, your patriotic donation will support our service members with warm meals, care packages, connections to home and so much more while they protect our freedoms.
**[Thousands of USO supporters are stepping up in the last days of 2025 to ensure that our troops have what they need in the year to come.](https://links.e.uso.)** Some of them do it because they have family deployed and want to care for them--others are former service members who remember how crucial the USO was to their well-being when they served and want to pay it forward. Others are patriots, recognizing that it is our duty to support our troops as they sacrifice so much for us.
Whatever your reason for giving to the USO, we are grateful -- and hope you will make another gift before the year ends.
On behalf of our service members,
The USO

name: Mark Lamb
subject: Running to challenge the political class
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS
body:
When something is broken, you don't complain — you fix it. |
---

[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=d9feed66fbee444c98e5c6e893bf1fab&_e=W8EecRQ8RlsjNc6NdN8rU-GiwJVTsuskk5cM4uxEAGx0BBiRAPwAkPuK-lNhRfMDq7vpAgl9IVx1Zcl4US4Qqb-3G7JJnqU7EReP38Xf1s4U1-IxGOvdKSeLbCdgULCT5dyDZZoftqxn1i7zWy_F1lFgHXnJrPURFCzUKoJaJK2wV9A4ba7DU_OqJFVlyyls-hA_decFSjZYeYTyw1M3CuO0V_1Nbpm51z9qDJB4GgMiSfJc-XhKFhMPVbYoZ50VHZuVL4tUUS8AjT4wK6fBWBZkvzsY-6GUZY_cHTDWnWvJIuwbN4WjFJrzwho94xsfcncUUWqQw6WwsC4giFPwNzdElCmhNHwfw-A1UWS0ASNFRtdNUu3xKqgwDde4N3qGHgYF_TYaToKtGNfOpbdtM3mw1TJ5jK0u6x9hqHq26xEQYbEumoXUkIGGrzammCuKywpPVaHA0S0s2mhWqkZd-G2JXYNJQwtoLZ-QZgb55DLAwGFihw0QQFciv9kodddGvX7PMLNJ6AOb97CPE0pB3VscV8KpX3gCjuewTpbVTZoA1_r-KjcZy7bAkVuP1FNq5jk_UbcoO9bPwF0jI53TJsPf6XaChgJVKB1PrOrHAHdvRZpmJmi0NqNO0mLamfbQKUTKdyxwKNjJuln7HhlDTG0mIm7lHifQ8CLK6pgZONU%3D)

Fellow Patriot,

Long before I ever put on a badge, I made a simple promise to myself:

When something is broken, you don't complain - _you fix it._

That philosophy guided me through eight years as Sheriff. It's why I stood up to cartels, why I defended our border communities, and why I backed our deputies even when it wasn't politically convenient.

But today, something bigger is broken.

Washington has stopped listening to the people who make this country work. Not the lobbyists. Not the bureaucrats. _The people_.

The ranchers who wake up before dawn. The small business owners who sign both sides of the paycheck. The parents who are fighting to protect their kids' future. The families who still believe in God, Family, and Freedom.

They deserve a voice that won't fold when things get tough.

**That's why I'm running for Congress - not to join the political class, but to challenge it.** To restore the kind of leadership that answers to the people, not to the unelected and unaccountable. And I'm going to fight for that every single day. But I can't do it alone.

If you believe America needs leaders who show up, tell the truth, and stand firm - **[I'm asking you to join this mission.](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=d9feed66fbee444c98e5c6e893bf1fab&_e=W8EecRQ8RlsjNc6NdN8rU-GiwJVTsuskk5cM4uxEAGx0BBiRAPwAkPuK-lNhRfMDq7vpAgl9IVx1Zcl4US4Qqb-3G7JJnqU7EReP38Xf1s4U1-IxGOvdKSeLbCdgULCT5dyDZZoftqxn1i7zWy_F1lFgHXnJrPURFCzUKoJaJK2wV9A4ba7DU_OqJFVlyyls-hA_decFSjZYeYTyw1M3CuO0V_1Nbpm51z9qDJB4GgMcxpD09p097cfCoDG0InalBEIsv4ij1uqk_e13ecm071Tlz0McGaM7F6lYX7n8Nd-VoIGaoC5JefITACHuDLZisAzxIQ0NK9yrgYhvYybxiPeBs6bMWVfcJFHIcV8fZ8tpCP-T6XuZEYCalEzS0adXV4mC_ehaO4l-dMVu5UOCl80QgHAL3KHqMYREAjoiTXVVsM2PU39Q9UG0wyrPdxQzI3rgPV5ooXzOBoglD8IDE9X-28veulIOZk_yeV5KkXqa8NZSXfcNnWNVFuwkT8QDIVmcg_1pAq10pdu_qGuzZRkIb54Hoe0_g6WB3QSzP07YsPkwifHxjp3U2Ca7ME5Gi5-__v1sEEsZh65bTJJ2mdthxTrF53IFPse8IVf6FDtAa6xzkAW7Fh-ssnwAYDM3PhU_z9QwMVreoCxr0p0ZDfWlGQQyy1bg3zI4DJckAt8%3D)**
