clean_body: When something is broken, you don't complain — you fix it. | Fellow Patriot, Long before I ever put on a badge, I made a simple promise to myself: When something is broken, you don't complain - you fix it. That philosophy guided me through eight years as Sheriff. It's why I stood up to cartels, why I defended our border communities, and why I backed our deputies even when it wasn't politically convenient. But today, something bigger is broken. Washington has stopped listening to the people who make this country work. Not the lobbyists. Not the bureaucrats. The people. The ranchers who wake up before dawn. The small business owners who sign both sides of the paycheck. The parents who are fighting to protect their kids' future. The families who still believe in God, Family, and Freedom. They deserve a voice that won't fold when things get tough. That's why I'm running for Congress - not to join the political class, but to challenge it. To restore the kind of leadership that answers to the people, not to the unelected and unaccountable. And I'm going to fight for that every single day. But I can't do it alone. If you believe America needs leaders who show up, tell the truth, and stand firm - Thank you for being in this fight, Mark Lamb Republican for Congress, AZ-5 Join Mark Lamb on social media! | | PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go: .

name: Marc Elias
subject: Two really important things:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Hey there, it's Marc Elias.

I'll get right to the point: [**This is a request for a donation of $5 to the DSCC ahead of their end-of-year deadline.**](https://go.dscc.org/go/22074?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251228-MarcElias-EliasDeadlineFR-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251228-MarcElias-EliasDeadlineFR-Pros-sl1&akid=22168%2E9716520%2EfgZN0T)

But please, **let me explain why this moment is so important.**

I've gone to court dozens of times to stop Donald Trump's lawlessness, and I'm proud to say that I've beaten him 64 times. That tells you two really important things:

**FIRST, Donald Trump and his MAGA minions _don't quit_.** If we don't want their extreme agenda, we'll have to stand up and stop them. And stop them again. And _again._ **Every single time.**

**SECOND, the GOP is _beatable._** We just have to put the work in -- and that includes at the ballot box.

That brings me to today. The DSCC is working to lay the groundwork for a MASSIVE blue wave in the midterms, and to get us in tip-top shape, they've set a $500,000 end-of-year fundraising goal.

**But as of now, we're on track to _fall short_ of that mark -- and unless we play catch-up immediately, we could be handing victory right back to the GOP.**

[**So now's the time to show up, because that's how we win -- take it from someone who's done this before. clean_body: | | | Hey there, it's Marc Elias. I'll get right to the point: But please, let me explain why this moment is so important. I've gone to court dozens of times to stop Donald Trump's lawlessness, and I'm proud to say that I've beaten him 64 times. That tells you two really important things: FIRST, Donald Trump and his MAGA minions don't quit. If we don't want their extreme agenda, we'll have to stand up and stop them. And stop them again. And again. Every single time. SECOND, the GOP is beatable. We just have to put the work in -- and that includes at the ballot box. That brings me to today. The DSCC is working to lay the groundwork for a MASSIVE blue wave in the midterms, and to get us in tip-top shape, they've set a $500,000 end-of-year fundraising goal. But as of now, we're on track to fall short of that mark -- and unless we play catch-up immediately, we could be handing victory right back to the GOP. We need your help to fight for the Senate. name: Do the math
subject: GOP on track to rig enough seats to win 2026 midterms
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Colorado Draws the Line
body:
| | | | | [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001I1qXX0F8F-JJ7OKGUjaTn_BabY-UOD6CyxByJxs97CLpK3N58eHWU4933_wJ0os7DvGYb3Lpgc-EP4vwxF20aE_YUk32NtoWvNRgbbHQu_sjBMFJAyaVXZsaNZxPu1DKij7nMY8WEUaWigjmR-L48iipnzPq2BOO-MgTCZYxDYTZuFMp2zote8tywGiLkyJKzOxgJ2nnL-dgwtbkxGJzCF8X-X4d0B7mE9MIFtX-o2k=&c=ukVsGvLkAFyRxl4BeHkl_8QRmg8WxQw7W3dEK-9M3OWuMJBvOQrpCQ==&ch=nl0FiRxTjr3ujbU3PmnXF99ndmOLhDDKUCpWUOUf-bia9EKrUYeISQ==)
---
Peter,
In 2018, the last time Trump faced a midterm election, Democrats picked up 41 Congressional seats. Based on the massive overperformances by Democrats in elections across the country this year, Democrats should be on track for an even bigger blue wave...
Unfortunately, MAGA now holds an edge in redistricting. If it continues at its current pace it won't matter how many seats we flip, we will be at an unbeatable disadvantage, and we won't reclaim the House.
That means it doesn't matter how unpopular Trump becomes, his rigging of the 2026 midterms ensures that he will no longer have to face the voters to maintain his grip on power.
---
| [](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001I1qXX0F8F-JJ7OKGUjaTn_BabY-UOD6CyxByJxs97CLpK3N58eHWU4933_wJ0os7IAMz_Tbl5LP5yIuoSTv-rNf1l67zQMR7gYiNxgucwupdi0b9DRmsF4RE1VMr6wBaunh3vRougmylwAc3hnhjtzVR1t5hkX0qnBkpP2x1h8wxRbDity92tgYEnOpTY7frzyjf3G58uqO4ECahDqpW1yp9f7wIfBEJJXrAol_SwxM=&c=ukVsGvLkAFyRxl4BeHkl_8QRmg8WxQw7W3dEK-9M3OWuMJBvOQrpCQ==&ch=nl0FiRxTjr3ujbU3PmnXF99ndmOLhDDKUCpWUOUf-bia9EKrUYeISQ==)
---
Mark our words, our system of free and fair elections is being torn apart at a pace so rapid that they will no longer exist by November 2026.
Our only shot at coming out of this with our Democracy intact is if we redistrict back. If done aggressively, not only do we survive but we beat them at their own game and ensure their plan backfires.
California and Virginia just joined the fight but if it's just them it will be too little too late. More blue states must come online with redistricts of their own in order for Democrats to stay on track to win the midterms and reclaim the House.
Colorado is now joining this fight. Colorado Draws the Line (formerly Fair Fight Colorado) is introducing the ballot initiative that will give Coloradans the opportunity to vote on new maps that will make Colorado bluer and counteract Trump's redistricting efforts.
But by no means is success in Colorado guaranteed. It will be a steep uphill fight. Getting this on the ballot will require collecting hundreds of thousands of signatures and we expect MAGA to contest us over every single one of them. They will throw everything they have into stopping this.
We need to prepare for all out war. [If every person on our email list gave just $5 it would mean hundreds of thousands of dollars for this critical endeavor. Small dollars add up. Can you make a donation of $5, or whatever amount is meaningful to you, to get Democrats the resources we need to redistrict Colorado? ](https://6s79z9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001I1qXX0F8F-JJ7OKGUjaTn_BabY-UOD6CyxByJxs97CLpK3N58eHWU4933_wJ0os7XyiyfD1ec3gq_hPf3rD1tb-lhcWKBJk-hu2YX3WnL-63UcEy5AHDxILlzopy5KpbHVC02uIkRfzoNpcH1wNKOy40pG3r_xNa7QUCiXrnJMadK3QOeamQLiST_Ct7Y9c65mu2Xr_KW1UCg5VAk8co2sLKPv3UtoPXLRr9GvJKDqMmy_YUMnzMoA==&c=ukVsGvLkAFyRxl4BeHkl_8QRmg8WxQw7W3dEK-9M3OWuMJBvOQrpCQ==&ch=nl0FiRxTjr3ujbU3PmnXF99ndmOLhDDKUCpWUOUf-bia9EKrUYeISQ==)
---

name: James Talarico
subject: I want to be radically transparent
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
I promised you from the start that I would run this campaign with radical transparency. So I want to take a moment to pull back the curtain in a way most U.S. Senate candidates would not.
Running within a rigged system doesn’t mean we have to give into what’s broken. We can reject the corruption of corporate PAC money and make clear that we are not going to sell our values to the highest bidder.
But it requires a whole lot of us digging deep to give whatever time or treasure we can spare if we want to take back Texas.
That’s especially true with our first full quarter FEC deadline on Thursday. The only way we hit our goals is with lots of people giving. Folks like you are the backbone of this campaign, and you deserve an honest look at what it will take for us to succeed.
Right now, it means reaching 7,000 donations before midnight to stay on track. It’s a steep goal, yes. And I promised radical transparency, so here it is.
The thermometer below is linked directly to our ActBlue and will update every few minutes. Everybody will be able to watch how we’re tracking. You can see for yourself whether we beat it or fall short.
[](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251228-1-ak&t=1&akid=348%2E1347274%2EKfjEBG)
**[Can you please make a donation of any amount and push us one step closer to taking back Texas? This FEC fundraising deadline is important. But if we each give a little, then we’ll hit our goal before you know it.](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251228-1-ak&t=2&akid=348%2E1347274%2EKfjEBG)**

Showing these numbers requires vulnerability. But building a movement that can take back Texas requires trying something new.
We’ve seen where the DC playbook (sponsored by billionaires and corporate mega-donors) gets us. It fails the people and upholds a broken status quo.
The only way I want to run — and the only way I think a Democrat can flip Texas — is with a true grassroots movement of the people and for the people.
Am I nervous that we’re a long way from this goal right now? You bet. If we fall short, everyone will know.
But I owe you — the people funding this movement — honesty. Too many politicians forget there are real humans on the other side of these emails. They treat it like a transactional relationship. But I won’t.

clean_body: Peter — I promised you from the start that I would run this campaign with radical transparency. So I want to take a moment to pull back the curtain in a way most U.S. Senate candidates would not. Running within a rigged system doesn’t mean we have to give into what’s broken. We can reject the corruption of corporate PAC money and make clear that we are not going to sell our values to the highest bidder. But it requires a whole lot of us digging deep to give whatever time or treasure we can spare if we want to take back Texas. That’s especially true with our first full quarter FEC deadline on Thursday. The only way we hit our goals is with lots of people giving. Folks like you are the backbone of this campaign, and you deserve an honest look at what it will take for us to succeed. Right now, it means reaching 7,000 donations before midnight to stay on track. It’s a steep goal, yes. And I promised radical transparency, so here it is. The thermometer below is linked directly to our ActBlue and will update every few minutes. Everybody will be able to watch how we’re tracking. You can see for yourself whether we beat it or fall short. Showing these numbers requires vulnerability. But building a movement that can take back Texas requires trying something new. We’ve seen where the DC playbook (sponsored by billionaires and corporate mega-donors) gets us. It fails the people and upholds a broken status quo. The only way I want to run — and the only way I think a Democrat can flip Texas — is with a true grassroots movement of the people and for the people. Am I nervous that we’re a long way from this goal right now? You bet. If we fall short, everyone will know. But I owe you — the people funding this movement — honesty. Too many politicians forget there are real humans on the other side of these emails. They treat it like a transactional relationship. But I won’t. #
Friend, Elon Musk has now begun funding Republican House and Senate campaigns ahead of the 2026 midterms – a major shift after months of posturing about third parties and “independence.” 🙄 | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122117292/591637072/-185804085?refcode=em-12.28.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMjIzMDc0ZS0wNmU0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IUg_da4ane3oRkdAmmLrc6iIW-v29E1YaM0EYynZtk4=)

* Kristen was the most targeted Democrat by Musk’s super PAC last cycle, which spent _$1 million_ on attack ads against her.

* Kristen saved the Saginaw Social Security office from Musk’s DOGE program, which threatened to close it down.

* Kristen co-sponsored a bill – named the **MUSK Act** – which works to keep people like Musk from directly influencing the West Wing.

Let’s be clear: Elon Musk is throwing his billions around to reshape Washington in his image. And if there’s one Democrat who’s proven she won’t back down, it’s Kristen – which means she’s likely to be one of his top targets again.

name: StateDemocrats.com
subject: What is one thing giving you hope right now?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
#

Yesterday, we asked supporters like you what frustrates them the most about the state of politics in America right now. Today, we’re shifting gears to focus on something different: **What is one thing giving you hope right now, Peter?** **[SHARE YOUR THOUGHTS HERE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98974?t=1&akid=9877%2E614464%2EMuO4La)** Whether it’s the momentum we’ve seen for Democrats lately (like the big wins up + down the ballot back in November) or even just a conversation you had with a loved one during the holidays, we’d love to hear about something that has given you hope for 2026.

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Dangerous decisions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
WaPo - Opinion: A frantic warning from 100 leading experts: Our democracy is in grave danger [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122825_1&amount=25]
We’ll keep this short, Team: those experts are right.
The attacks of the last few years in our state legislatures across the country targeting the right to vote, especially minority communities, are an assault on our democracy.
Now Trump, DOGE, and the MAGA movement want to privatize our postal service and harm mail-in voting , which has helped millions of Americans vote -- not just in the pandemic, but going back to the states that adopted the widespread practice in the early 90's.
“ Our entire democracy is now at risk ,” they wrote. “ History will judge what we do at this moment. ”
With so much at stake now -- not just with voting rights -- we've got to push our efforts to fight back into overdrive ASAP.
Team, none of this happens without all of us doing our fair share. Step up to fuel our work with $25 or whatever you can today to try and stop the destruction of our government, save our democracy, and protect the right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122825_1&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_1&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_1&amount=50]

https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122825_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122825_1&amount=20]

name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: Before Charlie was taken… he wrote this...
body:
This is what gave Charlie strength
Charlie’s final message—support Turning Point USA & receive his last book.
| | [ ](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=5c268760abc3488fae48053446897aaf&_e=1Xx6XRcgZU6sKBIMF4gvbslPZORyc29YjF4BOTF_0_dOFJoOsc57GZUzPHSrO8p5mF2aQmELTErZgO3yYEbDPZLTEHhPmxr5gZf-KX-LbIraDbFNO5bvLSuy9q5BpqjGF6z0AkISQnDX2fsMojTU4EPsbz1k3WDqEKWzmEQka_661Z4_Zbc40rYTp71M6713M8wUEPJ9hARH4nFsfRoXeN9S3zw0iQmUQN4nTPZarHSJnn0yPyJleJbAw7raBzo0ZS9RKs5tlkFAQznVq2pfxKr3BPcs281t-fdxWawUyGl7H60nDz-Fc6QhPgUX1Qo2DrPcFuVdKWcbU3TucvbkuCHpcAJAMJt9Ds9t9gSdKLdlBjg5YkNu4u8rrlmXPeYtqaMb4rkT6He2ooS83gbgCw7vm1-1TkCHB7Aa3xo4Wo2gFGs0mF99zP0LoVcXY02v81tfgVYEgH8TUg_hfuJby8ulNtIid3eg3YY1jjCiet0kFMCj6KK4AfaWWcLFjb-ePPrMjWBjVYCO2r0WVKsNgNYdL1x2pIGkvf380bIthz0q_nHD0cTbQiUZAwWSrkV0fxJgnohG2_uVTgB9dAGmzyHRR3xhvEE2R84kJ0Upj9_oR43ismSELTITHp334hUfuM9_h4eCB_fD5ISBKjsO_5ywyaMWkx6ld7W6Ea8NXbDFs3az08wBJqU0xgzB-UJl)
---
| Fellow Patriot,
---
| Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud.

He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus.

He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness.

And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible.

I saw it up close -- _Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. _

But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything.

_**He started observing the Sabbath. **_

Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance.

_But as an act of obedience. _

As a way of saying, **_" God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." _**

Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped.

He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield.

No email. No media. No work.

He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children.

It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much.

But over time, it changed him.

And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned...

clean_body: This is what gave Charlie strength Charlie’s final message—support Turning Point USA & receive his last book. | | | Fellow Patriot, | Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud. He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus. He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness. And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible. I saw it up close -- Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything. He started observing the Sabbath. Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance. But as an act of obedience. As a way of saying, " God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped. He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield. No email. No media. No work. He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children. It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much. But over time, it changed him. And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned... | | This was Charlie's final message. His final act of trust. May the message he left behind lead you -- just as it led him -- to a deeper trust in God... A stronger love for your family... name: Demand Justice PAC
subject: Behind closed doors at the Supreme Court →
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251228-61026&t=1001&akid=61026%2E1407376%2ETXoGw3 ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20251228-61026&t=1002&akid=61026%2E1407376%2ETXoGw3 ]Demand Justice PAC

Trump is actively hacking the legal system to hammer in his extremist
agenda through the Supreme Court's emergency docket.

The emergency docket is for emergency cases. It’s in the name.

Yet Trump has the Supreme Court so far in his back pocket that he’s turned
it into a loophole — a fast-track to override lower courts, dodge
accountability, and get whatever outcome he wants, whenever he wants it.

If Trump doesn’t like a ruling, he doesn’t follow the law. He runs to the
Supreme Court and demands an instant decision in his favor behind closed
doors. It’s manipulation plain and simple.

And while shadow docket rulings are supposed to be temporary, their
consequences are anything but. It’s nearly impossible to undo mass
deportations, firings without cause, and racial profiling in immigration
cases.

This is how authoritarian power consolidates — quietly, quickly, and most
alarmingly, with the Court’s blessing.

Demand Justice is calling this out relentlessly. We’re exposing these
abuses and making it impossible for the Court to rubber-stamp
authoritarian power grabs. But this fight only works if our grassroots
movement is strong enough to confront a captured Court. So we’re asking:

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251228.QNsW5B&refcode=em20251228-61026&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61026%2E1407376%2ETXoGw3 ]Will you stand with Demand Justice today by pitching in
$5 right now to help us force transparency, raise the
political cost of these rulings, and defend the rule of law?

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20251228.QNsW5B&amount=5&refcode=em20251228-61026&t=1022&akid=61026%2E1407376%2ETXoGw3 ]Donate $5 ››

No president gets to rewrite the rules, defy the courts, and rule by
emergency decree.

In solidarity,

Demand Justice PAC

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.demandjustice.org/survey/less-emails?t=1023&akid=61026%2E1407376%2ETXoGw3

Unsubscribe: https://act.demandjustice.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?email=%%email%%&t=1025&akid=61026%2E1407376%2ETXoGw3
Email us: feedback@demandjustice.org

611 Pennsylvania Ave SE, #192, Washington DC 20003

name: Jon Ossoff
subject: You probably know what this is about
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

Hey friend, it’s Mark Kelly. My friend Jon Ossoff is running for Senate in Georgia and needs our help. I hope you’ll read the message below from his team, and [**rush a split donation between Jon's campaign and mine.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-ossoff.o6kuHF&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=1&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)
---
I think you know what this is about.
I’m coming up on our final FEC fundraising deadline of 2025.
So if you are ready to help my re-election campaign before this year comes to an end, please, split a donation of whatever you can between Mark Kelly and me here: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mek-em-ossoff](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-ossoff.o6kuHF&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=2&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)**.
A strong showing on this FEC report proves to Trump and MAGA (and their Super PAC allies, including Elon Musk) — that our movement in Georgia is thriving and unstoppable.
**So here’s my promise:** You can count on me and my team to spend the money we raise this year fighting like hell to win next November.
But here’s the thing: I need to be able to count on folks like you to contribute at the moments that matter. **And this deadline really is one of them.**
[**I certainly don’t expect folks to give every time, but if you have anything you can spare to help my team end this year strong and meet our goals before the FEC deadline at the end of this month, I would really appreciate it if you would split a donation between me and Mark Kelly.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-ossoff.o6kuHF&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=3&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1396?c0=mek-em-ossoff.Xq-TZr&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=22&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1397?c0=mek-em-ossoff.n-TdNG&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=23&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1390?c0=mek-em-ossoff.U96K3E&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=24&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1391?c0=mek-em-ossoff.OprxVC&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=25&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1392?c0=mek-em-ossoff.BYnxB7&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=26&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1395?c0=mek-em-ossoff.OpjaJE&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=27&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)**
---
****
I don’t love asking for money. I don’t think _anyone_ does.
But the reality is I have to. I can’t win without you.
_Citizens United_ opened the dam for limitless dark money to flood into elections.
_That’s a big part of why Congress is broken and corrupted. It’s why we have really expensive elections._
And also it’s why campaigns like mine have to build serious grassroots fundraising operations from Day One.
I don’t love asking for money. I don’t think anyone does.
_I need your help, friend!_
**I am literally MAGA’s #1 target.**
They will spare no expense and try every dirty trick.
Yes, this election will be tough.
But I know how to win tough fights. I’ve done it before.
Let’s do it again.
[**Please, consider making a contribution to Mark Kelly and my reelection before our critical FEC deadline at in 10 days. Every donation counts. Each one sends a powerful message to MAGA that we’re ready to fight.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-ossoff.o6kuHF&refcode=em251228-1-90&t=28&akid=6313%2E6688874%2E7AL37M)

Thanks for being part of this.

I hope you’ll read the message below from his team, and I think you know what this is about. I’m coming up on our final FEC fundraising deadline of 2025. So if you are ready to help my re-election campaign before this year comes to an end, please, split a donation of whatever you can between Mark Kelly and me here: . A strong showing on this FEC report proves to Trump and MAGA (and their Super PAC allies, including Elon Musk) — that our movement in Georgia is thriving and unstoppable. So here’s my promise: You can count on me and my team to spend the money we raise this year fighting like hell to win next November. But here’s the thing: I need to be able to count on folks like you to contribute at the moments that matter. And this deadline really is one of them. name: Adam Schiff
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| I’ll never take your support for granted, Derek.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9342%2E5257348%2ELys3rI)
In just a moment, I’m going to ask you to **[make a contribution](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q42025_abs_em.dd-KUe&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20251228_AM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=4&akid=9342%2E5257348%2ELys3rI)** before my final fundraising deadline of the year. But before I do, I want to take a moment to _say thank you._
Through every challenge, every headline, and every attack thrown my way (of which there are many), you have stood unwaveringly by my side.
I want you to know that I never take your support and confidence for granted.
And while the attacks on our work to uphold the values we share keep mounting — and will only continue to do so in the new year — your continued support motivates me to never back down from this fight.
**For the sake of our democracy. Our Constitution. Our future.**
Donald Trump and his allies will continue to come after me, and anyone else who stands up to him. They hope that their persistent attacks will silence and intimidate others from holding Trump’s corrupt regime accountable.
But I’m not backing down. I never have, and I never will.
Your support enables me to fight back even harder — not only against the attacks we’ve already faced from Trump and his allies, but against the challenges to come.
**[I’m so grateful to have you on this team, but heading into 2026, I’m asking you to have my back once more. I have a critical FEC deadline in just a few days, and it would mean a lot to earn your support. Will you pitch in $10 or whatever you can to help us reach our year-end goal before the deadline?](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=q42025_abs_em.bOXSWp&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20251228_AM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=5&akid=9342%2E5257348%2ELys3rI)**

name: Powered by People
subject: Sharing some interesting stats with you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240566%2E444211%2EvVdodG)
Earlier this month, we asked our supporters one simple question: _What matters most about Powered by People and the work we do?_
The answers were consistent—and they’re exactly what drives how we organize, fight, and win:
* **90%** of respondents said that registering voters is one of the most important things our organization does.
* **85%** said grassroots organizing and community building is essential as we head into 2026.
* Staying in touch with newly registered voters to help them navigate voter suppression was also a top priority.
* Most respondents are already focused on Congressional and statewide races, especially the big races for Governor and U.S. Senate.
* And when asked what actions supporters would consider taking with Powered by People in 2026, **55%** said helping register new voters and **44%** said volunteering in person at events.

Here’s what these numbers reveal: **the path to victory is powered by people.** Winning isn’t about fundraising dinners or flashy TV ads—it’s about registering voters, showing up in communities, signing up and deploying volunteers, building long-term power, and supporting races that matter up and down the ballot. That’s what Powered by People does every single day.
Right now, ahead of 2026, the easiest and most impactful way to get in the fight is to chip in so we have the resources to run our proven programs. Your contribution doesn’t just fund a campaign—it fuels organizers, strengthens communities, and makes sure every newly registered voter has the support they need from now until election day.
This is the work that wins elections. This is the energy that wins in 2026. And it starts with people like you stepping up.
[**So, what do you say? Can we count on you to power this movement today with a $3 donation to Powered by People?**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-1.YLwOmm&refcode=ema20251228-240566&t=3&akid=240566%2E444211%2EvVdodG)

Thank you,
Powered by People

#

$3.
This is the amount Gabby is asking you to pitch into GIFFORDS PAC before our end-of-year fundraising deadline in just 3 days. We need a surge in donations today to stay on track with our ambitious goal and continue our fight to end gun violence.
If that’s all you need to hear, you can use this secure link to donate:
**[go.giffords.org/go/DecemberDeadline?refcode=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-q42025.UDsFRy&refcode=em251228-1-b&t=1&akid=11136%2E3935704%2Er801wD)**
If you need a few more reasons to make that donation, we’ve got you covered:
$3 because Donald Trump called himself the NRA’s “best friend” before the election — and now, his administration is helping his gun lobby CEO buddies profit.
$3 because House Republicans are preparing to push through a nationwide concealed carry mandate, which would make all of us less safe. We’re working to build the largest mobilization program since Donald Trump took office to fight back, but we need more donations.
$3 because our fundraising numbers will be released to the public soon. Our gun lobby adversaries will try to find any sign of weakness, so we need you to help us report the most amount of donations possible.
If everyone reading this message chipped in $3 right now, we would hit our fundraising goal within the hour. But not everyone can, which is why we’re asking you to make your first donation before we ring in the new year.

name: =?UTF-8?B?U2VuYXRvciBCcnljZSBSZWV2ZXM=?=
subject: An Important Update on My U.S. Senate Campaign
body:
Peter, After deep reflection, prayer, and many conversations with my family, I have made the difficult but necessary decision to withdraw from the United States Senate race. A serious family health matter, combined with the realities of timing, requ
| | | [](https://xbdizvxp.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Fbrycereeves.com%2F/1/0100019b65c4faf2-9d732db0-bf86-4130-b2b3-5cae7becd088-000000/4gEdt3t6wc5_Vyim9nwqBgKxjgo=458)
---
| Peter, After deep reflection, prayer, and many conversations with my family, I have made the difficult but necessary decision to withdraw from the United States Senate race. A serious family health matter, combined with the realities of timing, requires my full attention at home. My faith and my family come before all else, and I will always honor that calling. I have always lived by a simple code: honor God, love my family, and protect this great country. That code guides this decision today. My commitment to the people of Virginia has never wavered. I remain fully dedicated to serving the citizens of the 28th Senatorial District, and I look forward to seeking re-election to my Senate seat in 2027. The people of my district elected me to be their advocate, their voice, and their problem-solver, and that mission continues with renewed focus. In the coming General Assembly session, I will remain deeply engaged in advancing key legislation essential to Virginia's future, safeguarding our AAA bond rating, strengthening our economic competitiveness, and ensuring that our Commonwealth remains the nation's gold standard for supporting our military, our veterans, and their families. These priorities demand disciplined leadership and unwavering attention, and I intend to give nothing less. This past election cycle was devastating for Republicans across our Commonwealth. We suffered historic losses, and we now face a void of leadership at the very top of our party. If we are to return to greatness, we must end the back-biting and internal warfare that weakens our cause. It is time to reflect, regroup, and unite. I want to extend my heartfelt thanks to every supporter, volunteer, donor, and friend who devoted their time, energy, and belief to our effort. I am not disappointed. I am simply prioritizing what comes first. Your commitment to our cause remains a source of strength and encouragement, and I will continue standing beside you as we work to restore principled, conservative leadership in Virginia. The road ahead will be challenging, and this General Assembly session will be tumultuous. But I will remain where I am needed most, fighting for my constituents, defending our values, and working every day to build a stronger future for Virginia and for the country we love. We ask you to please keep our family in your prayers and respect our privacy as we go through this difficult time.
---
| Bryce ReevesVirginia State SenatorRepublican for U.S. Senate, VA
---

name: Truly dark
subject: “I love hitler” - Boebert’s world view trickles down to Young Republicans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
These comments did not happen within a vacuum. Lauren Boebert bears responsibility for hateful and violent language—her poisoned world view is trickling down to the next generation of Republicans.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pHosZOI2rP4Oa2iQiezGac--tzz2EhH392z8_sj6FuaFxZ4Rzu4H2YR5knzS9ycxkQrEIoLfy-YM_KO955_A5GMeTB7iPNKBDe82QihYujJhV6fKKfNoYF5bWVtmz1CCjVlH3WSHbk2_MGs-_SEMYE9rGJwYnk1PLOFvZK-teCovIMVdh3ooPHzSXt7UtqMVIHI6yfrhkLeVOTWHldpBxUjrE0zsoLfq&c=S7T1O_3XLhFCl9GlZmeyB2GycvIvCdUCxKYh5Ipolqht-77BgJuoUg==&ch=N9bB79_7u4YB9k6XktedFy8EenFlb_4zLGK4R9mpNPnQQcnSSpWS7w==)
---
|

Peter,
The text messages of dozens of the GOPs brightest rising stars were just exposed and what they said is disturbing.
They called for rape, violence, and for their opponents to be sent to gas chambers, expressed their adoration of Adolph Hitler and Nazism, and called Black people monkeys and the N-word.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pHosZOI2rP4Oa2iQiezGac--tzz2EhH392z8_sj6FuaFxZ4Rzu4H2YR5knzS9ycxkQrEIoLfy-YM_KO955_A5GMeTB7iPNKBDe82QihYujJhV6fKKfNoYF5bWVtmz1CCjVlH3WSHbk2_MGs-_SEMYE9rGJwYnk1PLOFvZK-teCovIMVdh3ooPHzSXt7UtqMVIHI6yfrhkLdHQqHqbZ2Uz0SqkA6_yRFj&c=S7T1O_3XLhFCl9GlZmeyB2GycvIvCdUCxKYh5Ipolqht-77BgJuoUg==&ch=N9bB79_7u4YB9k6XktedFy8EenFlb_4zLGK4R9mpNPnQQcnSSpWS7w==)

These comments did not happen within a vacuum. Lauren Beobert bears responsibility for this hateful and violent language--her poisoned world view is trickling down to the next generation.
Since her election to Congress Lauren Boebert has called her muslim peers jihadis, demanded a black Congresswoman be sent back to Africa and called a Black, disabled Congressman with a cane a pimp, used genocidal rhetoric against trans people, accused Jews of conducting recon of the Capitol, and so, so much more (the list could go on forever but we have work to do).
Young Republicans are taking note and are following Bobert's lead.
We are on the cusp of something horrible happening here in America and Lauren Boebert is at the tip of the spear pushing for it. She needs to be stopped before it is too late and I am running against her to do exactly that.
[Please, can you rush a donation of $50, $25, or even $5, to help me cut this rhetoric off at its source, flip this district, and take back the House for Democrats?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pHosZOI2rP4Oa2iQiezGac--tzz2EhH392z8_sj6FuaFxZ4Rzu4H2YR5knzS9ycxj4BZfrkzu5B4kLB86ZSGZhLL5IGW9Ixvc-yLFICi1Atk4737tSr6Onh8kqxgct3GAIETSgFR3i79aq39oWcur2s3JvpSLz8Xx5NQN8OEojd5BFe3J_PPeIr8cOoJ3TovHllXE3N5vkLPnYD8bQG76J7SqTXl_fBZEhTXipC6nz8=&c=S7T1O_3XLhFCl9GlZmeyB2GycvIvCdUCxKYh5Ipolqht-77BgJuoUg==&ch=N9bB79_7u4YB9k6XktedFy8EenFlb_4zLGK4R9mpNPnQQcnSSpWS7w==)

body:
I will never take your sacrifice for granted.
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=894a0ea1f03d45c9905caa59d8e222fb&_e=l6qLBKs7xyvtWMhqofzgL-okAT-yeXYH6M1JzVJgq0F0xZNfmHPHq2pw7EUVacVB6PrSa7I30nW0kCVT4RxOsY9qhcNeQiEGJtzh5r0R8DuJvMNFmvc5tdRSmCttSCssSVvwiXVemGAWr6JaBpkmZKnItslO1_eNJWXXJ8vp84cCIBC79TKD2I3sQA1KiBRcVN9PD8WwcTb4wZMP7OCRlZ9_9XwfEQXhiNJoftCLUPABh4NSMjfgtQRUTJrb8U_bsyJnwG0PIQITxtxyi6335F2w7QWAddbnxjj3DgP4eiyaBeOmGvLrOatpsn2JzHRGH_Ghny63HmiA7SAtiiWo-Vt4obec3IIfo5DZy1fIlNpfLHEnPFZsYPfAZeB80FWkzG3flEcQKY1UkDnhkPcAlAOmDWEWDl6jRC9wmL_FpH-m3LaL21TXFbMlVQvY3YSnpGgoGH1_D8dVjJXzFz0DFWcuOaHDsWMUkXKvCAAXPG7HncshoKZ9bwS8LCkIBPXVKC7sMfMDJxe0VyiFpddoz3vyGHWjcpfnOxYNN8CFOPbtuOphnpwXda3x8EqgfNlsoBnS_JZsvG15zx5BD2W--Yvdg4-D2CUwnZ7d1o-MPVAkxR0F3-WhKcR1MVMwHlnCMwuC2UOR_LvEfHJyv8Rnp7XMYzfwU9Df7GSYapqbPrCbU7K79Ba8TeEfe3TlJnyYYtKgXWd_pMhPnOmKwvXevnyEbDz1sygKBI4JU64U0S22ruZnwB7uHRWOW8UcbyZT2C5B-_kovKA7XGDxThsL3c3r0GBOpAbhKGvxighJl8AV-fDLvzh6_WKjd2W2f-j1UmmXAkBDRG2B11sAv4nSV2lST_0mFOrJQ3KPd8CeBxsujLwv2mAoZXXlGZ5qFy_8yHqcfYXqnbnvyxOMua8GTYKGevgl2zXqmY0eXFqtVGw%3D)Politics today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks.
**But I want to cut through the noise for a moment. **
[I’d like to tell you a quick story.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=894a0ea1f03d45c9905caa59d8e222fb&_e=l6qLBKs7xyvtWMhqofzgL-okAT-yeXYH6M1JzVJgq0F0xZNfmHPHq2pw7EUVacVB6PrSa7I30nW0kCVT4RxOsY9qhcNeQiEGJtzh5r0R8DuJvMNFmvc5tdRSmCttSCssSVvwiXVemGAWr6JaBpkmZKnItslO1_eNJWXXJ8vp84cCIBC79TKD2I3sQA1KiBRcVN9PD8WwcTb4wZMP7OCRlZ9_9XwfEQXhiNJoftCLUPABh4NSMjfgtQRUTJrb8U_bsyJnwG0PIQITxtxyi6335F2w7QWAddbnxjj3DgP4eiyaBeOmGvLrOatpsn2JzHRGH_Ghny63HmiA7SAtiiWo-Vt4obec3IIfo5DZy1fIlNofg1wna_OwdlT9Ttp4bUa8q3X99-DwsvSiGwXIG6oTtfPxWoacw41djT5x7tpSF53RhBeYZ_FTgTcvOOIAdqH9cJZrlGFk7qvTlN5gSIW47pDy6VwsLVoG4wwod9bTEd8MZ5GaatoNntxx5MUzLKejyBDGqu0rM8rCP2RUuwHxUxq1Iauk0tNQkVxizlQT0teq7L0Ic01ce0eNuW94PuadPLyjuZhgqeMKtcQbiiHuxhnsRnCVUeaHa-w4B9MylqZeCZ8N2y5AdgCD6S0xThi6cBluxIIUEM3HlJtuFvHuTxUfSQULrwOrc0J7yCWHikAv_5MCWo0RDGeLpfu85Jafs5a8cUXckwfoS0rwxTBObOjR7tLlScKf-5BZzpKqRpF8Kka8tA3BLVSSxECjFntmczFxwtWJa7xPbKk49naAGpsNZt_6SSbrazv_2PgPK3x4l9EkHSAH3TU7l19ddXJXQ4hiLwoj8VpafXmvoVAaM-SpB2eUOHDlWuBT-PfS9a_q0zumcko0HKW4FNdEQ6PYbXjzYskyaj3ugazQ27PHUZcAxqLlrjKsz_ydrC2F5rU%3D)
I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and **I know what sacrifice looks like**. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice.[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=894a0ea1f03d45c9905caa59d8e222fb&_e=l6qLBKs7xyvtWMhqofzgL-okAT-yeXYH6M1JzVJgq0F0xZNfmHPHq2pw7EUVacVB6PrSa7I30nW0kCVT4RxOsY9qhcNeQiEGJtzh5r0R8DuJvMNFmvc5tdRSmCttSCssSVvwiXVemGAWr6JaBpkmZKnItslO1_eNJWXXJ8vp84cCIBC79TKD2I3sQA1KiBRcVN9PD8WwcTb4wZMP7OCRlZ9_9XwfEQXhiNJoftCLUPABh4NSMjfgtQRUTJrb8U_bsyJnwG0PIQITxtxyi6335F2w7QWAddbnxjj3DgP4eiyaBeOmGvLrOatpsn2JzHRGH_Ghny63HmiA7SAtiiWo-Vt4obec3IIfo5DZy1fIlNq7rJjNae_cA806J8B0wMZZC2ABc_Uo8p2KaDKcyHttpVnU1tjhLGaRswF1KBt7N7nluevzFTBl3pR_autsQZUz53wOA__2uygrP3WCNSGw2ZQtedVTmpS8WugaZQJUSy9ej082KZNNgcpIz8IbIHDMeqXinuRFlahBnIOKaJ-9sRIJUcin8m4qFigjjl4nxl7zUjaar9ey_YAwS35ffvDosOK6ah8v-maM-h_Snwpfh_ox07a5BVvXzqV92TXIxVFQ1t-SxOAbcw9rPCRVwVAhmeg_YDCb_C1QrYgqq2Yzf6xF-GNtwPJb4mu0Fhh0LZ5Y-Le11kp4U8Ig9Bn1OgZaG912zNfy9cirK33yAn3ULYNLZCsB5_xPxdI6FgJzkO-kMWcZHMb5dxv8dqv2HAPPgUhPipQLjhg_EXyWfX0gebr6tC8tI8tunk_dK2Odx6ZpAPWcsKVKvoIOzcB3K9dwF2o93pEyMcXysSsLEdC2kECOkIq9_kSo0-_xVa1s8tA5QoinBXdBpFbivKF3hRWj8lrjCVMG2cLZNEL43ZISWTscOf_68jd9tCP61n8VdWY%3D)That's why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't **“just money.” ****
**It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. **
**So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: **
**[THANK YOU, Peter!](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=894a0ea1f03d45c9905caa59d8e222fb&_e=l6qLBKs7xyvtWMhqofzgL-okAT-yeXYH6M1JzVJgq0F0xZNfmHPHq2pw7EUVacVB6PrSa7I30nW0kCVT4RxOsY9qhcNeQiEGJtzh5r0R8DuJvMNFmvc5tdRSmCttSCssSVvwiXVemGAWr6JaBpkmZKnItslO1_eNJWXXJ8vp84cCIBC79TKD2I3sQA1KiBRcVN9PD8WwcTb4wZMP7OCRlZ9_9XwfEQXhiNJoftCLUPABh4NSMjfgtQRUTJrb8U_bsyJnwG0PIQITxtxyi6335F2w7QWAddbnxjj3DgP4eiyaBeOmGvLrOatpsn2JzHRGH_Ghny63HmiA7SAtiiWo-Vt4obec3IIfo5DZy1fIlNqJBQQjfqTizU90-bvKGfNpsN_UlKiQU3mDUrCktf5XcA_uUPXu6plSJ8M1MCEdlcxz8AotHXZ1TOu7PiRJhOGPIUMXC-jO7PY6-IoX63Ap6vT7ALuIMMQdzQsD45VFn0AHIEDUZovBAkEklliD3SgY79YABZ8x0tCe2iiqwe-2vHyb7fsKZcdWkHz5pGqwLt4tgz51AdEuMAMAU20YoHUKPL-riRF9ZFIVgFhnemEIstefcPh9ni7dl0z2SOVM2V9h5CtEmXf_bcXvg2CNT2i3j2sB-bmyVumY59OS81MvW0pPux4AAezY7Q7kn5RWZyDTLVSbFvpOvH4Iwqbw_RaLPeJOpNhvmLe_u5lqMAgrGszwNFWM3mTPgK-7RLbEtJe95qvfdtU8hk1OZEGogf6GnD_-OWHQ1WSOCK0GaakAyRD1ASKjNHC2FuL2sErvu3dsv1EvOyJ0fA8rKsIXWyG1cvGb4ZCif4rGcA-6SD9YS6nihcYF-mYDLfe3ghTTnOWVoRdSGaBj9noAv2YITdVf9axlNQ01AFywUDGLlfCQh-PBqIVD-2_pyvF2Ze6Fkos%3D)**
**We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country.**
**I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were[ ONE ELECTION AWAY](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=894a0ea1f03d45c9905caa59d8e222fb&_e=l6qLBKs7xyvtWMhqofzgL-okAT-yeXYH6M1JzVJgq0F0xZNfmHPHq2pw7EUVacVB6PrSa7I30nW0kCVT4RxOsY9qhcNeQiEGJtzh5r0R8DuJvMNFmvc5tdRSmCttSCssSVvwiXVemGAWr6JaBpkmZKnItslO1_eNJWXXJ8vp84cCIBC79TKD2I3sQA1KiBRcVN9PD8WwcTb4wZMP7OCRlZ9_9XwfEQXhiNJoftCLUPABh4NSMjfgtQRUTJrb8U_bsyJnwG0PIQITxtxyi6335F2w7QWAddbnxjj3DgP4eiyaBeOmGvLrOatpsn2JzHRGH_Ghny63HmiA7SAtiiWo-Vt4obec3IIfo5DZy1fIlNoaJJD3L0vLxmhBtv2s7XuGOo79X9n0FM4VoR48vmfhIUWQZOG_qVrd6xw-2FO6ccKT1MeMB9a9RQs4DZKPQU0HJbLXw8M-CrdNSqPl4QCoYpbwUTEzV9cc5S5yT0YJZLoTUVn1fr_Z4sEz6SEGNqdf0x94f9DwUrVN_y0PLZP-76iSstEYB5KkylR58s9wkIxdOeV6ANEFQPXhPZIjDUfgk-lQYZb0iSXPy5eftHd59OFFOgyuvcVeaGd4or4LPUn_m52Zg-TMMAlONRPWcXLuZxemErLLCkirPuDe3PEClMjnSdsc25De8rlp-BWYYbS6hmYJAUjGbqCYCcwxj7j6TUEfGKVxZy7LsV-LIIb1wFB_9TRosU1sSIrjaLVm6--z2x_ISZQGMobW4jk81wHxRhpJ0hZwCZ5ozCSy7RxfxXq-e5AQt3yrO2q-Acp4pNJrJOlDhOtlh2twtGQG84J2v0xtwBDZpGd1lihhXuwXiGn30sTcaQWnRDV2-uKygZuXFskwcu4Avef959StkOjPvBMU4aBA9mg4n-23w23cvbwmIc6L0YZaWn9xwj1lpNU%3D) from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore.**

I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice. why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't “just money.” It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country. I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. # [Happy paw-lidays from Bailey!](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6380051&post_id=182628428&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyNjI4NDI4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjY5Mzk0NzcsImV4cCI6MTc2OTUzMTQ3NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTYzODAwNTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.X50714thuPxi8F_VAWB6js2TlC8CM609TlaM23pk9JA)
### A holiday dog check-in from Michigan's snowy sidewalks with love, gratitude, and new smells to report
| | [Haley Stevens](https://substack.com/@haleyformi)
---
| Dec 28
---
| [](https://substack.com/@haleyformi)

_**WOOF! WOOF!**_
Human person! It's me! Bailey!
I'm the official campaign dog of Haley Stevens for Senate (self-appointed) and semiprofessional snow explorer. ❄️🦴
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a426a61a-0d96-4dc0-bb68-0c98e9458a8b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I hope your holiday was full of warm treats, belly rubs, and all the things that make your tail wag. 🐕 I just wanted to send a quick note before the New Year, straight from the snowy sidewalk here in Michigan, to say: **Thank you.** 💙
This time of year is special. Even _I_ can feel it - and I spend most of my time thinking about treats and squirrels. 🍖🐿️
Christmas was a blast over here! 🎄 I got a brand-new squeaky toy, a peanut butter bone the size of my head, and my mom _may or may not_ have caught me sneak-eating the Christmas ham. YUM. No regrets. 🍗👅
I've been ruff-ing it out on the campaign trail with my mom (she's Haley's finance director!), and I've seen just how much heart, hustle, and hope this campaign is made of. From long meetings (I nap through those, obvi 😴), to canvass launches, to organizing events where _so many nice human people_ pet me behind the ears - this grassroots team is magical. ✨
I love them. I know I love everybody, but I _reeeeeally_ LOVE them. 🥹💕
But honestly? It hasn't all been treats and chew toys. 🦴
I know a lot of families have had a tough year. I've overheard my mom and her friends talking about folks worrying about grocery prices, heating bills, and health care. That stuff is hard. 🥺 And it doesn't go away just because the holidays are here.
But that's why I love this campaign so much. Because I've seen Haley work her tail off for humans all across Michigan. She listens. She shows up. She fights for families (and their dogs). 🐾
And if _you_ had a hard year, I just wanted to say: **You 're not alone. **I'm sending you so many snuggles and kisses right now!! 💖🐶
From the bottom of my puppy heart: **Thanks for being part of this team.** 💗
I'll be back with more paw-litical updates soon. But for now, I'll leave you with this photo of me braving the winter winds - nose to the breeze, tail held high, fighting for democracy and the last patch of un-sniffed snow. 🌨️🐕
Wishing you a cozy New Year full of treats, walks, naps, and good friends. 💫
Happy paw-lidays!
Love you!
Bailey 🐾**
** _On behalf of my mom Katelyn, Haley, and the whole Team Haley pack_
* * *

A personal note from Haley 👋

I'm a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. I've spent my career fighting for Michigan's working families - and now I'm running for the U.S. Senate to keep standing up for the people I love.

But here's the truth: Senate control could come down to Michigan. And Trump and his megadonors know it. That's why they're gearing up to spend tens of millions to defeat me and hand this seat to a MAGA Republican.

I need your help to fight back. This race will be one of the most competitive - and expensive - in the country, and I'm counting on grassroots support to win.

If you're with me, will you chip in today?
[Donate](https://substack.com/redirect/c18b57bc-88d9-454b-a8bb-b1ede9c41a54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

clean_body: A holiday dog check-in from Michigan's snowy sidewalks with love, gratitude, and new smells to report | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more A holiday dog check-in from Michigan's snowy sidewalks with love, gratitude, and new smells to report | | | Dec 28 | | | | | | WOOF! WOOF! Human person! It's me! Bailey! I'm the official campaign dog of Haley Stevens for Senate (self-appointed) and semiprofessional snow explorer. ❄️🦴 | | ---|---|--- I hope your holiday was full of warm treats, belly rubs, and all the things that make your tail wag. 🐕 I just wanted to send a quick note before the New Year, straight from the snowy sidewalk here in Michigan, to say: Thank you. 💙 This time of year is special. Even I can feel it - and I spend most of my time thinking about treats and squirrels. 🍖🐿️ Christmas was a blast over here! 🎄 I got a brand-new squeaky toy, a peanut butter bone the size of my head, and my mom may or may not have caught me sneak-eating the Christmas ham. YUM. No regrets. 🍗👅 I've been ruff-ing it out on the campaign trail with my mom (she's Haley's finance director!), and I've seen just how much heart, hustle, and hope this campaign is made of. From long meetings (I nap through those, obvi 😴), to canvass launches, to organizing events where so many nice human people pet me behind the ears - this grassroots team is magical. ✨ I love them. I know I love everybody, but I reeeeeally LOVE them. 🥹💕 But honestly? It hasn't all been treats and chew toys. 🦴 I know a lot of families have had a tough year. I've overheard my mom and her friends talking about folks worrying about grocery prices, heating bills, and health care. That stuff is hard. 🥺 And it doesn't go away just because the holidays are here. But that's why I love this campaign so much. Because I've seen Haley work her tail off for humans all across Michigan. She listens. She shows up. She fights for families (and their dogs). 🐾 And if you had a hard year, I just wanted to say: You 're not alone. I'm sending you so many snuggles and kisses right now!! 💖🐶 From the bottom of my puppy heart: Thanks for being part of this team. 💗 I'll be back with more paw-litical updates soon. But for now, I'll leave you with this photo of me braving the winter winds - nose to the breeze, tail held high, fighting for democracy and the last patch of un-sniffed snow. 🌨️🐕 Wishing you a cozy New Year full of treats, walks, naps, and good friends. 💫 Happy paw-lidays! Love you! Bailey 🐾 On behalf of my mom Katelyn, Haley, and the whole Team Haley pack * A personal note from Haley 👋 I'm a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. I've spent my career fighting for Michigan's working families - and now I'm running for the U.S. Senate to keep standing up for the people I love. But here's the truth: Senate control could come down to Michigan. And Trump and his megadonors know it. That's why they're gearing up to spend tens of millions to defeat me and hand this seat to a MAGA Republican. I need your help to fight back. This race will be one of the most competitive - and expensive - in the country, and I'm counting on grassroots support to win. If you're with me, will you chip in today? Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate. | | | (C) 2025 Haley for MI 33717 Woodward Ave #539, Birmingham, MI 48009 ? ?

| | Friend, Everyone in politics loves to brag about endorsements. _Senators. Congressmen. Influencers. Party leaders._ And yes – Bryce Reeves could go out and get every big Republican endorsement in the country if he wanted to. But Bryce asked us to send this email because the endorsement he wants first is **YOURS.** Not Washington’s. Not Richmond’s. **YOURS.** Because this campaign isn’t going to be won by insiders – _it’s going to be won by the people who believe in Bryce’s mission, Bryce’s integrity, and Bryce’s fight for Virginia._ That’s why we’re launching the _**Official Endorse Bryce Petition**_**,** and Bryce wants **YOUR** name on it before anyone else’s. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

A Tesla Explosion That Changes Everything

Near Trump Tower in Las Vegas, a Tesla Cybertruck exploded—and it’s raising serious questions.

The details are _shocking_. Gas canisters. Fireworks. A planned detonation.

But here’s the twist...
| [ ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=9d5994cd6ad1424e8a32107d11efa082&_e=fFCC65uGeZRw4ishMJdnEXOxh-paxi35kb9VtN8ipTVNZ3-DmEkqNPJAT7gbIFVRESAOK8YUF53yTqWavZ6kFT7jtoRAVq_97NzQG5Dr9DpVmMVxzFcm_ur7wAw3URMdPCbfbW8-P9xFfyf8qQ33sQHf3UehSFdwV6Yd8wK5vouOkGcDrl72qL0LO3stDW_eSn_VpHZGcaF2UYG_Cf08ZNEYA1QCZJjdhSOm-FCvVMiBWmWl6jHWk5Rx4R6BrMIS0dpq-EcXKqzMlukzvKK9HwH-5eR4BjCpkyga1ItlQCAe8FOhMR3NJozw-peW5tP9DW43q6x5dth-X-7cxjl2RbpfE693G8t_LmY0DmOZFri1iP2v0eo8tnCJrjVONMsjwTi_D9S1tCOIUvbYr9a4UNMEDF12X7gDaegGv1OjIOxtTn2aTjbGIYEcz0WyhlQZ1IjjUe5FDxCSPMOevGfbby2zrc2PVCg8fmDlJ26VLbs4izlakQgD0OsDQOn8Px3fD9GFIKvqbr5Ffx5RRjOJ1xznj8EEss19ICrv9cBUr-IJijDSGRMItvDuOamXoG6CuMt9sIpcEa9FH4J5BTL_wA%3D%3D)
---
There’s something bigger behind this story—something most people haven’t noticed.

Do you see it?

[CLICK HERE to uncover the truth.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=9d5994cd6ad1424e8a32107d11efa082&_e=fFCC65uGeZRw4ishMJdnEXOxh-paxi35kb9VtN8ipTVNZ3-DmEkqNPJAT7gbIFVRESAOK8YUF53yTqWavZ6kFT7jtoRAVq_97NzQG5Dr9DpVmMVxzFcm_ur7wAw3URMdPCbfbW8-P9xFfyf8qQ33sQHf3UehSFdwV6Yd8wK5vouOkGcDrl72qL0LO3stDW_eG9qZ3ADVmGiOdwmL1zfnwJsk1zlf5aK04PuVasGGQ-E03LBxnjXrDXIITdbfh5K1t85R4WOZCXuLTEhboa_9oj6fSn_TONXOoOdZxa4TRNmMOlI4hs4WvdIy1rFxni5i1Abb2MP1uOqYVpxkmOpQXdy0bHwMUYGZPanPn-zfh4kgXF-j06wKxFdFLryxwrXeisBs7mLTT1lGESuDsh6Hin5nsL9J3NyvqRFP0l-Qf9zhH0BhSpSs2D6_LFzJXW1mPHWB89Upw4PjSbQdo89VETObBIUMkhAWCQcisUqqFUbvKjImX0gE7NmR0xl1Z5QRr1fIXNJWvFmDljXtrBIyQNV6YNssWE5rnsnjKmASdwPjW9NhPgtoscuyay9ZWx5rBVlo2fC0smZiITV7WCUpyA%3D%3D)

You won’t believe what’s really going on.
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Near Trump Tower in Las Vegas, a Tesla Cybertruck exploded—and it’s raising serious questions.| | | | December 28 | | | | | Shocking Details Behind the Tesla Incident | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. The details are shocking. Gas canisters. Fireworks. A planned detonation. But here’s the twist... | There’s something bigger behind this story—something most people haven’t noticed. Do you see it? You won’t believe what’s really going on. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. body:
This is the opposite of how government is supposed to work.
#
After the disaster that was DOGE, followed by the very messy and very public breakup between Elon Musk and Donald Trump, Musk has announced he’ll be funding GOP House and Senate campaigns yet again in the 2026 midterms. 🙄
[](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1363?c0=mcb-em-20251228-1.P-yj6R&refcode=em2025MMDD-1&t=1&akid=2614%2E21716%2EM3QjpX)
---
We’ve seen how this ends already: Musk spends _millions_ bankrolling Republicans, and then uses his influence to slash federal jobs and programs that working people rely on, all while asking for bigger and bigger handouts for his own interests.
It’s the _opposite_ of how government is supposed to work. And if we want to beat Elon and his fellow GOP billionaires, we need to build a movement powered by the same working people whose interests they’re working against.
**That’s why we’re asking for your support right now.** [**With the midterms just around the corner, can Sarah count on you to pitch in $5 to help us reach our end-of-year goal and win back the House in 2026?**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1184?c0=mcb-em-20251228-1.wvW7Wg&refcode=em20251228-2614&amount=5&t=2&akid=2614%2E21716%2EM3QjpX)

In solidarity,
Team McBride

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
