name: Robert Garcia
subject: Add your name: It's time for immigration reform >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Robert Garcia for Congress
body:
This is personal for me
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122140506/591696465/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1N2E0ZTEzMi0zN2U0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vS9RI6nqQHMh8MVbOms3DVd4FY6fIHwPbasAdrKhdSo=)
Hi, it's Robert Garcia.
As a proud and patriotic immigrant, I want every kid to have the same shot I had — the shot that allowed me to give back to this country.
But today, millions of kids and families are being denied that same chance. So I’m asking you to stand with me and demand real, comprehensive immigration reform.
[ADD MY NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122140508/591696467/-1909961190?refcode=251228_RG_personal-pet-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1N2E0ZTEzMi0zN2U0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vS9RI6nqQHMh8MVbOms3DVd4FY6fIHwPbasAdrKhdSo=&emci=c9fe35e1-04e4-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=57a4e132-37e4-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=16877361)
My mom brought me to this country not knowing English and without an education. But thanks to a progressive immigration law passed by Congress in the 1980s, we were able to apply for permanent legal residency. I became a U.S. citizen in my early 20s.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122140510/591696469/-1909961190?refcode=251228_RG_personal-pet-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1N2E0ZTEzMi0zN2U0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vS9RI6nqQHMh8MVbOms3DVd4FY6fIHwPbasAdrKhdSo=&emci=c9fe35e1-04e4-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=57a4e132-37e4-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=16877361)
_**That act of Congress changed everything. **_
America gave me the opportunity to go to school, to serve my community, and to become the first LGBTQ immigrant ever elected to Congress. I know my story isn’t typical, but it should be. I believe every family should have access to that same pathway to success.
Right now, Congress should be an institution that helps people — not one that blocks progress or plays politics with families’ futures. But we’re facing big, structural challenges:
* Generational poverty
* Concentrated wealth and power
* Attacks on democracy
* Systemic racism
* And a fringe minority inside the GOP is determined to stop any meaningful immigration reform

I’ve seen firsthand that coalitions can be built — even when the opposition is entrenched, ideological, and loud. We can do big things when we insist on justice and fight for opportunity.
**That’s why I need you with me. Congress cannot keep punting on this. Families cannot wait. clean_body: This is personal for me = Hi, it's Robert Garcia. As a proud and patriotic immigrant, I want every kid to have the same shot I had — the shot that allowed me to give back to this country. But today, millions of kids and families are being denied that same chance. So I’m asking you to stand with me and demand real, comprehensive immigration reform. My mom brought me to this country not knowing English and without an education. But thanks to a progressive immigration law passed by Congress in the 1980s, we were able to apply for permanent legal residency. I became a U.S. citizen in my early 20s. That act of Congress changed everything. America gave me the opportunity to go to school, to serve my community, and to become the first LGBTQ immigrant ever elected to Congress. I know my story isn’t typical, but it should be. I believe every family should have access to that same pathway to success. Right now, Congress should be an institution that helps people — not one that blocks progress or plays politics with families’ futures. But we’re facing big, structural challenges: Generational poverty Concentrated wealth and power Attacks on democracy Systemic racism And a fringe minority inside the GOP is determined to stop any meaningful immigration reform I’ve seen firsthand that coalitions can be built — even when the opposition is entrenched, ideological, and loud. We can do big things when we insist on justice and fight for opportunity. That’s why I need you with me. Congress cannot keep punting on this. Families cannot wait. Kids cannot wait. ** Thank you for believing in a more compassionate, inclusive, and hopeful America. Robert Garcia = = * Paid for by Robert Garcia for Congress Robert Garcia for Congress 65 Pine Ave #348 Long Beach, CA 90802 United States This email was sent to . name: Pete Buttigieg
subject: The closest Senate election in the country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for SenateThis email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail
body:
#

[
---
Peter — it’s Pete Buttigieg. We are living through a time in the life of our country when freedom itself is on the line — and few races will test that more directly than Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign in Georgia. **I’ll share more in a moment. But if you believe, as I do, that hope can be the consequence of action, one meaningful step you can take today is to support Jon’s campaign using this link:** [**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ossoff-em-dec25?refcode=Pete**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251228-2-f&t=2&akid=1321%2E5691347%2Efi3e5R) Right now, the same forces working to consolidate total power in Washington have made Jon their top Senate target. They’re prepared to spend whatever it takes to replace Jon with someone who will help them undermine the freedom of working people. They need to replace Jon with one more Senate enabler of this administration’s abuses of power. Let’s be clear about what that means. When the tools of government are used to make people more afraid, more angry, and more alone — it’s serving the opposite of the government’s true purpose. A well-run government enhances freedom — from fundamental freedoms like the right to speak your mind, to the economic freedoms that come from access to health care, and an affordable everyday life for your family. That’s the kind of freedom Jon Ossoff stands for. And he represents the kind of leadership we cannot afford to lose in the Senate. **Jon’s election couldn’t be more important: There is no path to retaking the Senate that doesn’t run through Georgia.** The consequences of this election won’t just be felt in Washington or even Georgia — they’ll show up across America in the lives of people who need their government to stand up for their freedom, not take it away. I don’t say this lightly, and I wouldn’t ask if it weren’t urgent. This is one of those moments that will define what comes next: for Jon’s campaign, for Georgia, and for our country. And it’s going to take all of us. [**That’s why I’m asking: Would you please join me in supporting Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign by making a donation of any amount today?**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251228-2-f&t=3&akid=1321%2E5691347%2Efi3e5R) Gratefully, — Pete

name: James Carville
subject: Ungodly amount of money
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
We have to get our country back on track – and that starts by helping Sherrod win in Ohio.
#
Friend, it’s James Carville.
I’ve worked on a heck of a lot of campaigns. Over the past 50 years, I’ve won – and I’ve lost – tough elections across the country. So believe me when I tell you:
**Sherrod Brown’s Senate race in Ohio is SO DAMN IMPORTANT, and the only way he can win it is with YOUR support – right here, right now.**
**[Please chip in every dollar you can spare to Sherrod's campaign and State Democrats Committee. He just got back in the fight, and they need folks like you by his side ASAP.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98977?refcode=em20251228-9878&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9878%2E614464%2Eh5tYVx)**
We’re at a critical moment in American history. It’s like nothing I’ve ever seen before. Politicians in D.C. are raising costs, cutting veterans' benefits, closing rural hospitals, adding $3 trillion to our national debt – the list goes on.
We’re moving further and further away from being anything close to democracy. It’s freakin’ absurd!
_We have to get our country back on track – and that starts by helping Sherrod Brown win in Ohio._
Sherrod has spent his life fighting for workers and families, and he’s got the moral courage to stand up to the powerful special interests that are making life harder and more expensive for everyday people.
**But outside spending groups will pour an UNGODLY amount of money into this race to defeat Sherrod (I’m talking hundreds of millions of dollars) – and it’s on us to give him the resources to fight back and win.**
**[Folks, if anyone can flip Ohio’s Senate seat, it’s Sherrod Brown. But he can’t do it alone – nobody can: Will you chip in $5 or even $25 right now, split between Sherrod Brown and State Democrats Committee to power his campaign forward and send him to the Senate?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98977?refcode=em20251228-9878&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9878%2E614464%2Eh5tYVx)**

name: Mallory McMorrow
subject: Why I’m running for U.S. Senate
body:
[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/83?t=1001&akid=3437%2E898137%2EL0EJq0 ][IMG]

I'm Mallory McMorrow, and I'm running for the U.S. Senate. Point blank, we
need new leaders in Washington who will call out the bullshit.

[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/4?c0=mam-em-welcome2025.q2Jvqj&refcode=AU_MAM_FR_WEL_GEN_NTL_202512_PM1_V1_S1&t=1002&akid=3437%2E898137%2EL0EJq0 ]I’m going to share more about why I’m running. But before I do, if
you’re like me and are done with the BS coming from Washington, please
contribute to my campaign today.

Now I want to tell you a story. I never thought I’d be in politics. But
after the 2016 election, like a lot of us, I was fed up. Politics should
be about service, not self-interest. And not building up your own power by
tearing others down. So I googled “how to run for office.”

Seriously.

I took on a state Senate race in Michigan that no one thought we could
win. I took on an incumbent state Senator who refused to do anything about
the epidemic of gun violence, who sided with corporate polluters over
protecting our clean water, and who didn’t represent us.

Democrats and Republicans counted me out because I was young. Because I
had never run for office before. Because it “wasn’t my turn.” But through
the help of over 500(!) local volunteers, I swung a Republican-held state
Senate seat 20 points and flipped a district that included Mitt Romney’s
hometown. That victory sent me to the Michigan State Senate.

[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/4?c0=mam-em-welcome2025.q2Jvqj&refcode=AU_MAM_FR_WEL_GEN_NTL_202512_PM1_V1_S1&t=1003&akid=3437%2E898137%2EL0EJq0 ]My approach in the Michigan State Senate is the same approach I’ll take
in the U.S. Senate: no bullshit.

The system is broken, and we should call it out. I’m a millennial. On the
whole, we’re the first generation to do worse than our parents. For
millions of people, it can feel like you’ve done everything right —
working hard in school, going to college or earning a skill or trade,
taking on one job, then a side-hustle, then a gig, building your entire
life around work and still barely getting by. It’s impossible to buy a
house. It’s too expensive to start a family. We look at our parents and
wonder where it all went wrong. The American Dream feels like a distant
past.

Democratic messaging usually sounds something like this: “Democrats are
fighting to help you put food on the table.”

Oy. Now look, of course that’s true, but that vision isn’t big enough. No
one aspires to just get by. We need to think bigger, dream bigger, and lay
out a vision for the New American Dream that people can see themselves in,
something they want to vote for.

Because being against Donald Trump is simply not enough.

It’s about a Michigan where you know you’ll have a tight-knit, vibrant
community, where you’ll have restaurants, small businesses, and places to
go. It’s about creating a future where if you work hard, you will be able
to afford the life you dreamed of — to start a family, to buy that house,
rent where you want, have the car you want, have a fulfilling career, and
yes — maybe even have that little place Up North because you can afford a
vacation and time away to reset. And still put money away for retirement,
savings, or a college or house fund for your kids.

[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/4?c0=mam-em-welcome2025.q2Jvqj&refcode=AU_MAM_FR_WEL_GEN_NTL_202512_PM1_V1_S1&t=1004&akid=3437%2E898137%2EL0EJq0 ]Democrats have to get real. The status quo simply wasn’t working. But
while Donald Trump and Elon Musk want to burn it all to the ground, we
have to build something new. Something that actually works. For everyone.

I’m fed up with Democrats in DC who have completely missed how much people
were struggling. I’m fed up with the party of “Well, actually.” When we
told our party leaders that groceries and gas were too expensive, that
people were struggling to find the job they really wanted, that they felt
stretched too thin, what did Democrats do? Told them the economy was doing
great, actually.

So yes, even if the decisions made by our leaders staved off the worst of
a recession, people were still hurting. And we need to listen to them.

Voters know BS. I’ve called BS out in the State Senate, and that’s what
I’ll do as U.S. Senator.

[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/4?c0=mam-em-welcome2025.q2Jvqj&refcode=AU_MAM_FR_WEL_GEN_NTL_202512_PM1_V1_S1&t=1005&akid=3437%2E898137%2EL0EJq0 ]If you’re done with the same old nonsense, make a contribution to my
campaign and send a message to DC.

Right now, we’re at a crossroads. Donald Trump and Elon Musk are trying to
overwhelm us. That’s their plan; they want us to feel powerless. But we
are not powerless.

The reality is, there’s a lot in common between this moment today and that
moment in 2016 when I googled how to run for office. But what that means
is that we know exactly how to rise to this moment.

This is not a moment for weakness. This is the time to put our heads down
and get to work. That’s why I’m running for U.S. Senate. This moment, and
what happens next, is on all of us. I will never try to convince you that
I have all the answers. Just voting for the right person isn’t going to
fix everything. But together, I know we can show Michigan — and the rest
of the country — how powerful we really are. Let’s create the future we
want to see, together.

[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/4?c0=mam-em-welcome2025.q2Jvqj&refcode=AU_MAM_FR_WEL_GEN_NTL_202512_PM1_V1_S1&t=1006&akid=3437%2E898137%2EL0EJq0 ]So please join us in building that future: make a contribution of
whatever you can to my campaign for U.S. Senate.

[ https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/4?c0=mam-em-welcome2025.q2Jvqj&refcode=AU_MAM_FR_WEL_GEN_NTL_202512_PM1_V1_S1&t=1007&akid=3437%2E898137%2EL0EJq0 ]JOIN US

Thank you,

Mallory

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=3437%2E898137%2EL0EJq0

It’s about a Michigan where you know you’ll have a tight-knit, vibrant community, where you’ll have restaurants, small businesses, and places to go. It’s about creating a future where if you work hard, you will be able to afford the life you dreamed of — to start a family, to buy that house, rent where you want, have the car you want, have a fulfilling career, and yes — maybe even have that little place Up North because you can afford a vacation and time away to reset. And still put money away for retirement, savings, or a college or house fund for your kids. [ ]Democrats have to get real. The status quo simply wasn’t working. But while Donald Trump and Elon Musk want to burn it all to the ground, we have to build something new. Something that actually works. For everyone. I’m fed up with Democrats in DC who have completely missed how much people were struggling. I’m fed up with the party of “Well, actually.” When we told our party leaders that groceries and gas were too expensive, that people were struggling to find the job they really wanted, that they felt stretched too thin, what did Democrats do? Told them the economy was doing great, actually. So yes, even if the decisions made by our leaders staved off the worst of a recession, people were still hurting. And we need to listen to them. Voters know BS. I’ve called BS out in the State Senate, and that’s what I’ll do as U.S. Senator. [ ]If you’re done with the same old nonsense, make a contribution to my campaign and send a message to DC. Right now, we’re at a crossroads. Donald Trump and Elon Musk are trying to overwhelm us. That’s their plan; they want us to feel powerless. But we are not powerless. The reality is, there’s a lot in common between this moment today and that moment in 2016 when I googled how to run for office. But what that means is that we know exactly how to rise to this moment. This is not a moment for weakness. This is the time to put our heads down and get to work. That’s why I’m running for U.S. Senate. This moment, and what happens next, is on all of us. I will never try to convince you that I have all the answers. Just voting for the right person isn’t going to fix everything. But together, I know we can show Michigan — and the rest of the country — how powerful we really are. Let’s create the future we want to see, together. [ ]So please join us in building that future: make a contribution of whatever you can to my campaign for U.S. Senate. [ ]JOIN US Thank you, Mallory You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:

_Today, we're in the midst of a redistricting crisis — and our democracy needs to be actively defended every day. That's why All On The Line is now**Redistricting Action** — a grassroots movement built for the critical fights ahead. Thank you for being part of it._
We’re closing in on the end of the year, and we don’t have a 2025 grassroots donation on file for the record associated with this email address.
Here’s why it matters: Anti-democracy extremists are working right now to redraw maps in several states to take voting power away from communities of color, and to undermine fair elections across the country. Whether we’re able to effectively hold the line for democracy depends on what we do in _this moment_. We have a plan — we need your help to make it happen.

Friend,

There are just three days until our final FEC deadline of the year. What we raise between now and midnight on December 31st will be made public, and you better believe the GOP will be combing through every line looking for any signs of weakness.

**[We still have ground to cover before the deadline. Every $3 donation from our grassroots supporters helps us close the gap and brings our progress bar closer to 100%.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141065/591698476/-1516755731?refcode=em-12.28.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDk0ZWI2YzItMzhlNC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=cthKxWZUwKKqRGnTIX-2JRBvDa51zeQcutWfF9E20u8=)**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141066/591698477/-1516755731?refcode=em-12.28.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDk0ZWI2YzItMzhlNC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=cthKxWZUwKKqRGnTIX-2JRBvDa51zeQcutWfF9E20u8=)
Our GOP opponents have the backing of corporate PACs and deep-pocketed super PACs. We’ve got something better: a people-powered campaign that refuses a cent from Big Pharma or Big Oil.

CA-45 is one of the most competitive House seats in the country, and national Republicans are treating it that way. They’re eyeing this report to decide just how aggressively to target this seat in 2026. That’s why we’re doing everything we can to close the gap now, and we’re counting on your help to do it.

**[The GOP is watching our numbers closely and preparing to flood CA-45 with a tidal wave of money if they catch even the slightest sign we’re off track. We’ve got something better: a people-powered campaign that refuses a cent from Big Pharma or Big Oil. name: Voter Action Project
subject: The ability to protect our fundamental right to vote
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Democracy Docket: New Ruling Cuts Off Key Tool to Protect Minority Voters [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122825_3&amount=25]
Folks: An update from the federal 8th Circuit on one of the most dangerous anti-voter rulings we’ve seen in years .
A federal appeals court recently cut off a key avenue for private groups and individuals to bring racial discrimination claims under the Voting Rights Act (VRA). The ruling, which applies in seven states, further weakens the ability of our civil rights law to protect voters, and could lead to the Supreme Court settling the issue.
This goes back to an initial ruling from 2023, the impact of which is pretty simple to understand: That voters and voting rights organizations can no longer sue to overturn unjust laws under the Voting Rights Act. According to the 8th Circuit, and now confirmed on appeal, only the Department of Justice (DoJ) has the legal standing to sue states over voting laws.
With former Florida AG Pam Bondi -- a "big lie" spreading MAGA acolyte -- serving as Attorney General, it's clear that the DOJ is not going to protect voting rights over the next four years.
Team, we have to fight this ruling and make sure voters and advocacy groups have the right to defend voting rights in court. Please pitch in $25 or whatever you can to make every vote count, either at the ballot box or in court >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122825_3&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_3&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_3&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_3&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_3&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_3&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=122825_3&amount=35]
This is all about the legal term "private right of action". The 8th Circuit says it doesn't exist. That means the pro-voting lawsuits we've seen in states like Wisconsin, Georgia, Florida, and others nationwide wouldn't be possible anymore if the Supreme Court backs up this ruling.
We won’t sugarcoat this: This could be a crushing blow to many voting rights organizations across the country, ourselves included. If this new appellate ruling is not overturned by the Supreme Court, it could unravel all of the great work pro-voting organizations are doing across the country — and allow dozens of unjust, unconstitutional voter restrictions to stand.
Attacks like this on what remains of the Voting Rights Act are going to get worse over the next few years. As voter suppression laws become increasingly targeted and oppressive , our work to combat them must become smarter, stronger, and more accessible to everyone.
We can't do this alone. Help defend our right to vote with $25 or however much you can today and make every voice heard in our elections >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=122825_3&amount=25]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122825_3&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122825_3&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=mGQmVyLoaerPlsM2d5UnouivjqyXWhLJFNhv5qv4GuHAARLYTdvvY3cPcjrlXwubCvPCou3dgVm6st2mwMXVmj5u2%2Fcjd5sWCDoffc3GyWXBiv6i2BUMj3AQg5mn%2FaR2Hsjb7sAOvItiZ7AzHh8mt57UW5MKTyfrOZpV8y8YPK6qBq1p42nKqnOeDK3tbRM0PODg6s%2FMwo4MM2PrUvKC37Eg6y09WDtFTPv9l4mabwgolw6ehsOmXXinQ5mF2VU4JXgcJQvPgN9GA47wKdEucPunPx4M3WzoSbaut5lzOIyWbw%2FG0c2s0D%2Fjk54ux2X4%2B5vyG5FtUAvPz5vvn2O6328N864y2dEI101r8kBUYEmCiofMh%2FI7NAL%2Bakj0Y0ynpOj1ibiCMByjJX52uS3Pf3%2FJ23kepHRnNDJsT8U8lc0%3D .

name: Laura Friedman
subject: 🎱 What does pool have to do with politics?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Laura Friedman for Congress
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141233/591698994/-1798313451?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjcyNTRkNGItMzllNC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=gbrW_QT3w3dtnti414HSSuMLnZ92eD_SLsFXX_fIpfw=) Here's something you may not know about me, friend.

Back in my college years, and even for a bit during my producing days, I was a semi-pro pool player. 🎱
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141234/591698995/-1798313451?refcode=em-12.28.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjcyNTRkNGItMzllNC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=gbrW_QT3w3dtnti414HSSuMLnZ92eD_SLsFXX_fIpfw=)
Believe it or not, some of the most important skills I developed at the pool table I still use today as a legislator. I learned that people like playing mind games when they want to win, and being one of the few women in a male-dominated sport taught me how to be strategic -- _and not be intimidated._

I've learned to stay focused on what winning actually means: _delivering results._
* I've been a champion for efforts to improve street safety, increase affordable housing stock, and protect our climate;

* I led the effort to improve transparency and keep people safer from sexual harassment and retaliation in the state assembly;

* I banned toxic, cancer causing ingredients from personal care products, children’s clothing & toys, and mattress;

* I’ve even taken on online ticket sellers to ensure Californians can see their favorite performers without price-gouging.

[I'm in Congress because we need proven, effective leaders in Washington who have a track record of delivering results. So ahead of my FINAL FEC deadline of the year, I hope you'll join me by making a small contribution to my campaign.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141235/591698996/-1798313451?refcode=em-12.28.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM1MzYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZjcyNTRkNGItMzllNC1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=gbrW_QT3w3dtnti414HSSuMLnZ92eD_SLsFXX_fIpfw=)

clean_body: | = Here's something you may not know about me, friend. Back in my college years, and even for a bit during my producing days, I was a semi-pro pool player. 🎱 = Believe it or not, some of the most important skills I developed at the pool table I still use today as a legislator. I learned that people like playing mind games when they want to win, and being one of the few women in a male-dominated sport taught me how to be strategic -- and not be intimidated. I've learned to stay focused on what winning actually means: delivering results. I've been a champion for efforts to improve street safety, increase affordable housing stock, and protect our climate; I led the effort to improve transparency and keep people safer from sexual harassment and retaliation in the state assembly; I banned toxic, cancer causing ingredients from personal care products, children’s clothing & toys, and mattress; I’ve even taken on online ticket sellers to ensure Californians can see their favorite performers without price-gouging. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | **= | = ---|--- = | = = | =** Thank you, Laura | | Paid for by Laura Friedman for Congress Laura's campaign doesn't take a dime from corporate PACs and completely relies on grassroots supporters like you! To chip in to Laura's campaign = To learn more about Laura's progressive record of supporting our working families, please visit: We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . name: Tim Walz HQ
subject: We’re hoping to reach you before midnight on the 31st. Here’s why:
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
We can’t run this campaign without you.
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/195?refcode=em20251228-367&t=1&akid=367%2E34582%2EuGlFqq)
Peter,
Midnight on December 31st is our first and only public filing deadline of the year since we launched this campaign. So it’s critical that we reach our $100,000 fundraising goal.
Today, we’re asking if you can chip in $5 to help us get there.
**$5** will help our campaign fight back against an onslaught of attacks from Donald Trump and his chosen Republican candidate, Mike Lindell.
**$5** will help us knock on doors across Minnesota and get Tim’s message out through targeted ads.
But we can’t do any of that without you. This deadline is coming fast, and every donation between now and midnight on December 31st helps us hit our goal and show the strength of this campaign when it matters most.
**[Can you donate $5 right now to help us reach our critical $100,000 end-of-year fundraising goal? Every donation will help Tim win next year.](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251228_2.aQCHlW&amount=5&refcode=em20251228-367&t=2&akid=367%2E34582%2EuGlFqq)**

Thanks for all you do,
Tim Walz HQ

name: The DC Bugle

Peter – I have an unlikely story to tell you. I grew up as a racial, religious, and ethnic minority immigrant with 29 letters in my name. My family worked hard to achieve the American Dream, but we faced tough times and had to rely for a time on food stamps and public housing to get by. But thanks to these programs, a lot of hard work, and support from our community, we defied the odds and found a path to the middle class. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141378/591699431/527259264?amount=25&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.28_Defying-The-Odds_B2_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NDFmN2ZiMS0zYWU0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3MkDkuzUnU328ewvbQ8AKMeBNg18_GncuTlOFTZgJRk=) Look, there aren’t a whole lot of people with backgrounds and stories like mine in the halls of Congress. When I first ran for office, a lot of people told me that I didn’t have the right background or connections to win. It wasn’t the first time I was written off — but voters proved them wrong. **Together we defied the odds again – and I had the honor of being elected as Illinois' first Indian-American Congressman. ** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141379/591699432/527259264?amount=25&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.28_Defying-The-Odds_B2_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NDFmN2ZiMS0zYWU0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3MkDkuzUnU328ewvbQ8AKMeBNg18_GncuTlOFTZgJRk=) Now, I’m trying to defy the odds one more time. There’s a lot at stake right now — our democracy is under threat, ICE is running rampant, and working families are struggling. **You can bet that Donald Trump and his far-right MAGA allies don’t want to see me in the U.S. Senate. And they’ll throw _everything_ at me to stop us from winning.** That’s why I need to ask for your support, Peter. This is an uphill battle, but I know firsthand what defying the odds looks like — and I know we can do it again. [**_We have a major FEC reporting deadline coming up in just a few days, and we know that Trump and his MAGA allies will be watching closely to see the strength of our movement. If we’re going to defy the odds, win this race, and help flip the Senate, I need a major outpouring of support in these final days. Can I count on you, Peter?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141380/591699433/527259264?amount=25&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.28_Defying-The-Odds_B2_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NDFmN2ZiMS0zYWU0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3MkDkuzUnU328ewvbQ8AKMeBNg18_GncuTlOFTZgJRk=) | | [ DONATE $10 → ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141381/591699434/527259264?amount=10&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,250&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.28_Defying-The-Odds_B2_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NDFmN2ZiMS0zYWU0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3MkDkuzUnU328ewvbQ8AKMeBNg18_GncuTlOFTZgJRk=)

name: Tim Kaine
subject: 2026 planning
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP44/1/88924/images/Kaine-Senate_logo_ussenate.png]
Derek, I hope you had a safe and healthy holiday with loved ones and friends.
As 2025 comes to a close, I wanted to share my perspective on what we were able to accomplish this year, and what we might expect to happen in 2026.
When Donald Trump and JD Vance took office in January, I held a deep and sincere hope that they would be successful for all Americans. As far too many people in our country struggle, I still hold on to that hope — for those who rely on our President to guide the way as best he can.
I also knew in January that part of doing my Constitutional duty as a United States Senator would be to fight against an Administration making reckless, dangerous, and often lawless decisions that impact the American people.
I sat down with my Senate staff and we made a list of every possible tool at my disposal for pushing back — and this year, we’ve used just about every one of them. And we’ve had some success. We passed legislation in the Senate to roll back Trump’s idiotic tariffs on Canadian and Brazilian goods. We finally repealed the 1991 and 2002 “zombie authorizations” for the Iraq War.
And we got Republicans on the record about where they stand — they made perfectly clear that they don’t support affordable health care for the American people, and we’ll remember that come November.
My mantra remains: Advance everywhere we can, defend everything we must. As we make our way through this challenging time, I will continue to use that as my North Star in advocating for the best interests of Virginians and all Americans.
No matter what you’re feeling as this year comes to a close — fear, anger, hope, anxiety, despair, determination — know that there are still many strong and committed leaders in Washington who are determined to fight for you, and to fight for the most vulnerable among us.
Next year, we have an opportunity to elect even more of those strong leaders — to take back the Senate, flip the House, and break the GOP trifecta of power here in Washington.
I believe we can do it. Virginia’s major 2025 victories proved that. When we organize, when we build grassroots power, and when we show up with real energy — we can win. But we can’t rest on our laurels after VA’s successes — we need to prepare for a massive blue wave around the country, starting now.
So as we close out the year, I’m humbly asking: Will you contribute whatever amount is meaningful to you to support our work to win big in 2026? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=100]
OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Whatever challenges or successes lie ahead for our country, the fact is that we will face them together, as one people. I’m proud to be facing it all with you, and I promise I’ll never stop fighting for a brighter future for our Commonwealth and country.
Wishing you and your loved ones a happy New Year,
Tim Kaine
Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
1490-5A Quarterpath Road #272
Williamsburg, VA 23185
Emails like this one are a critical part of staying in touch during this important election year. We value your support, and are so glad to have you on the team. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/P5FfEbyeIEejNQSsVSzanQ2?unsubscribedata=%2FTDlBR%2FAF5bfWUnlPjNzER827FV1MUVfhiRg2Brg1dQsbfryuGFhY1teThio39WGYkPteV6VTiF3yTNuVNIOZDdUZSUS%2FL9GmvuklUtY6IZbuj2ncMvVoVUhqI0vY5jhd7RwxXupvyrG3h87TnknHtEe5rt65nUZauUyoFsQ%2BVTXLeZbxym3rglht9QprbftWslqBVGt29lFUhkWxDB%2FWhW8%2BnL%2BWUu%2B6GHN4bnGcPJ6BW4bB28wFinbvQbXl4qL%2FLwiWLNCU6uYc%2BHUaDdx231gjy8gOx1etwgPmQqvcyFcPWqqXmapVDvqsJD40JJI%2FNRtvhF9bk4TF5nGRh9eJhzmQi1NZMVQzrdGGHWF0gQ%2BcaKXeOOuEeX40F2hs%2BqrCA6tvq3EbtJq3KXbU3dUDncqzTCJlRPeC6yj67APJz0%3D .

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Friend, can I send you my recipe for Biscochitos?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
This time of year, I'm making a lot of cookies

| | | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/134?t=1&akid=6499%2E400544%2EXE-GF3)
---
Friend, one thing you might not know about me beyond the fact that I'm running to be New Mexico’s next governor, is that my first job was in a bakery in Southeast Albuquerque. I honed my work ethic in that bakery, and today I LOVE to cook and bake.
During the holidays, one of my favorite ways to show appreciation is by sharing something homemade. I make biscochitos, New Mexico’s state cookie, for friends, family, and my campaign staff. Baking them always brings me back to home, to my family, our traditions, and the values that shaped me.
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3179?t=2&akid=6499%2E400544%2EXE-GF3)
**For me, cooking has always been about more than food. It’s how I show care, build connections, and bring people together around a table. My grandmother woke up every day before the sun, and gave me my true love of cooking New Mexican cuisine. She taught me that service starts at home and extends outward to the community, and that everyone deserves dignity, care, and a seat at the table.
**
I’ve carried that lesson with me ever since. Back when I was in law school at UNM, I’d make green chile chicken posole for student gatherings. During the protests against the Dakota Access Pipeline, I cooked chile and rolled tortillas for the water protectors, and later on for my congressional colleagues during the 2019 winter holiday season, I shared red chile and tamales.
**Food has the power to bring people together. There is something unifying about sharing a home-cooked meal or baked goods, even with those you don't know well or don't always agree with.**
Since launching this campaign, I have traveled across the state – breaking bread with New Mexican families from all walks of life. I've sat at kitchen tables and heard all about people's struggles and their hopes for the future. New Mexicans want a governor who will fight for affordable healthcare, child care, public safety, housing, strengthen our schools, and protect our environment
[**That’s the kind of governor I will be.**](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3179?t=3&akid=6499%2E400544%2EXE-GF3)

Like making biscochitos, being that kind of governor takes patience and determination. It means listening closely, respecting every ingredient, and doing the work carefully, by hand, step by step, until it comes together the right way.
I am ready on day one to get to work. We will start by raising wages and making sure every community has access to affordable healthcare. We will make it easier for neighborhood markets to open, eliminating food deserts across our state, and for grocery stores to carry New Mexico grown food.

We will ban price gouging and make up for federal cuts to Medicaid so families can stay healthy. We will help kids read better and earlier. We will expand access to mental health programs, get drugs off our streets, and keep our communities safe.
**If we get the ingredients right, we can build a New Mexico where every family can thrive. That’s a recipe worth fighting for: [That is why I'm asking you to invest in this campaign before the year ends to make sure we have the resources we need to stand up to GOP attacks and win in 2026. When you click this link and donate, I'll share my family's biscochito recipe so you can have a little piece of New Mexican holiday joy to celebrate the new year!](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3179?t=4&akid=6499%2E400544%2EXE-GF3)**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $10 >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3180?t=5&akid=6499%2E400544%2EXE-GF3)

clean_body: This time of year, I'm making a lot of cookies | | | Friend, one thing you might not know about me beyond the fact that I'm running to be New Mexico’s next governor, is that my first job was in a bakery in Southeast Albuquerque. I honed my work ethic in that bakery, and today I LOVE to cook and bake. During the holidays, one of my favorite ways to show appreciation is by sharing something homemade. I make biscochitos, New Mexico’s state cookie, for friends, family, and my campaign staff. Baking them always brings me back to home, to my family, our traditions, and the values that shaped me. For me, cooking has always been about more than food. It’s how I show care, build connections, and bring people together around a table. My grandmother woke up every day before the sun, and gave me my true love of cooking New Mexican cuisine. She taught me that service starts at home and extends outward to the community, and that everyone deserves dignity, care, and a seat at the table. I’ve carried that lesson with me ever since. Back when I was in law school at UNM, I’d make green chile chicken posole for student gatherings. During the protests against the Dakota Access Pipeline, I cooked chile and rolled tortillas for the water protectors, and later on for my congressional colleagues during the 2019 winter holiday season, I shared red chile and tamales. Food has the power to bring people together. There is something unifying about sharing a home-cooked meal or baked goods, even with those you don't know well or don't always agree with. Since launching this campaign, I have traveled across the state – breaking bread with New Mexican families from all walks of life. I've sat at kitchen tables and heard all about people's struggles and their hopes for the future. New Mexicans want a governor who will fight for affordable healthcare, child care, public safety, housing, strengthen our schools, and protect our environment Like making biscochitos, being that kind of governor takes patience and determination. It means listening closely, respecting every ingredient, and doing the work carefully, by hand, step by step, until it comes together the right way. I am ready on day one to get to work. We will start by raising wages and making sure every community has access to affordable healthcare. We will make it easier for neighborhood markets to open, eliminating food deserts across our state, and for grocery stores to carry New Mexico grown food. We will ban price gouging and make up for federal cuts to Medicaid so families can stay healthy. We will help kids read better and earlier. We will expand access to mental health programs, get drugs off our streets, and keep our communities safe. If we get the ingredients right, we can build a New Mexico where every family can thrive. That’s a recipe worth fighting for: If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Thank you, friend. Happy holidays (and happy biscochito season!) to you and your loved ones. 💛 Deb P.S. Throughout my life, cooking has always been a source of comfort and a way to bring my community together — and bringing people together is exactly how we’ll win this race. | | | | |

GOP Rep. Laurel Lee **[fired back at Democrat Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez](https://substack.com/redirect/aaecd23d-792e-474a-ba80-38cfc2131726?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for attacking Republican efforts to reduce fraud, waste and abuse in government healthcare programs, and trying to protect benifits for those gaming the system.
"It's really simple!!"
**[Watch the congress clash here:](https://substack.com/redirect/aaecd23d-792e-474a-ba80-38cfc2131726?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[](https://substack.com/redirect/aaecd23d-792e-474a-ba80-38cfc2131726?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[ Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/aaecd23d-792e-474a-ba80-38cfc2131726?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# **Iran declares "total war" with US, Israel and Europe**
Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian stated in a Saturday interview with state media that **["we are at total war with the United States,](https://substack.com/redirect/ea96a8c6-70e2-4d66-95f4-9eb9b4299c06?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Israel and Europe," claiming "they want to bring our country to its knees" and describing the conflict as "worse than the one launched against us by Iraq" during the 1980-88 war, amid UN sanctions over nuclear weapons pursuit.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ea96a8c6-70e2-4d66-95f4-9eb9b4299c06?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Tehran-backed candidate likely to lead Hamas:** Palestinian politician Khalil al-Hayya, who is considered close to Iran and expected to have a "clearly adversarial stance toward Israel," is likely to beat Hamas chief Khaled Mashaal in an upcoming election for head of the terror group's political bureau, which could be decided in the coming days as a tenuous Gaza peace deal hangs in the balance.
* **Netanyahu to pitch Trump on new Iran strikes:** Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is planning to travel to Mar-a-Lago Monday to meet with President Trump for the fifth time since his second term began, where he is expected to pitch a plan for new strikes against Tehran, with Israeli sources claiming Iran is repairing air defense systems and rebuilding its ballistic missile program destroyed during June's 12-Day War.
* **White House believes Netanyahu sabotaging Gaza deal:** Top White House aides believe Netanyahu is sabotaging the peace framework in Gaza, with an anonymous official telling Axios "It's felt for some time like the Israelis have buyer's remorse" and that "the Israelis sometimes do things that make it even harder," as Netanyahu remains skeptical of the plan to demilitarize Gaza according to Israeli officials.

* * *
# **JD Vance and other GOP leaders blast Gov. Tim Walz after viral daycare fraud video exposes empty daycare centers**
Vice President JD Vance called the situation in Minnesota **["a microcosm of the immigration fraud in our system"](https://substack.com/redirect/369916f3-7dfd-40a1-8970-24f920a111e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after a 42-minute viral video by independent reporter **[Nick Shirley showed allegedly fraudulent daycare centers,](https://substack.com/redirect/78cab272-51f7-40b8-ac7b-a746192b8a6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** including one with a misspelled "Quality Learing Center" sign that received roughly $4 million in state funds despite appearing largely inactive and supposedly accounting for 99 children.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/78cab272-51f7-40b8-ac7b-a746192b8a6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Elon Musk and Trump Jr. amplify fraud allegations on social media:** Elon Musk accused the "radical left" of exploiting government programs to bring in immigrants to "win elections and turn America into a single-party state," citing "the Somali voting bloc in Minnesota, a state that historically had zero Somalis, electing Ilhan Omar to the US Congress," while Donald Trump Jr. urged people to watch the video, saying "This is what they're doing to your country with your tax dollars."
* **Scandal involves $1 billion in alleged fraud tied to Somali community:** The viral video emerged amid a burgeoning scandal enveloping the Walz administration that notably included at least $1 billion lost to alleged social services fraud largely tied to the Somali community in the Twin Cities, with a portion reportedly ending up in the hands of the Somali terror group Al-Shabab, prompting Rep. Mike Lawler to demand "folks need to be arrested and prosecuted and the Governor of Minnesota needs to be held accountable."

* * *
**[Together with Natural Health Response](https://substack.com/redirect/e4d5b8d2-1164-4fef-9bcc-3beb0d3db8b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **" Too old to live"? Terrifying medical AI hits American doctor's offices**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e4d5b8d2-1164-4fef-9bcc-3beb0d3db8b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## **What happens when an AI computer thinks you 're "too old to save"?**
That's the terrifying reality happening in exam rooms across America...
**As machines (not doctors) are deciding who gets care ... and who doesn't.**
A new jaw-dropping video takes you behind the scenes and exposes EVERYTHING.
**[SENIORS: Here's the one thing you must know to protect yourself from medical AI.](https://substack.com/redirect/e4d5b8d2-1164-4fef-9bcc-3beb0d3db8b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/e4d5b8d2-1164-4fef-9bcc-3beb0d3db8b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
P.S. **AI makes mistakes 45% of the time.** _And most of the time your doctor is NOT required to tell you he 's using AI._ Before your next exam, **[you must see this doctor's shocking warning.](https://substack.com/redirect/e4d5b8d2-1164-4fef-9bcc-3beb0d3db8b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
* * *
# **Red state governor saves taxpayers millions through "common-sense" reforms** \- Pay attention Tim Walz!
Indiana Gov. Mike Braun highlighted **[his state's success](https://substack.com/redirect/ea9648a3-4b78-4d33-973b-c99c51052e8e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in raking in hundreds of millions in Medicaid savings by identifying "low-hanging fruit" such as people who should have been on Medicare instead of Medicaid, individuals double-dipping across states, and pharmacies exploiting the 340B discount drug program, saying states can fix problems without waiting for Washington.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ea9648a3-4b78-4d33-973b-c99c51052e8e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Minnesota 's fraud crisis could exceed $9 billion since 2018:** A Wall Street Journal report estimated that fraud losses in Minnesota since 2018 could top $9 billion, with bad actors openly exploiting the system through a repeated cycle of establishing "sham" companies and submitting false claims, as Gov. Tim Walz acknowledged the problem is "on my watch" and issued a third-party audit while pausing payments for some services.
* **Braun contrasts business approach with Minnesota 's handling:** The Indiana governor, who ran a business for 37 years before becoming a U.S. senator, said the solution is "common sense" and contrasted his state's efforts with Minnesota, where "they just want the government benefits but don't do anything to run them like you do a business," arguing that states willing to act can fix inefficiencies better than the federal government.

* * *

body:
New polling shows this race in striking distance — we must act now.
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=4275%2E582294%2EbRR6T7)
**I’m Rachel Petri, the campaign manager for Chris Pappas for Senate.**
I’m writing with an important update on the New Hampshire Senate race — and an urgent request. We need your help to match the GOP’s growing firepower here in the Granite State. Please read about the situation on the ground below.
* * *
CAMPAIGN MEMO
TO: TEAM PAPPAS SUPPORTERS
FROM: RACHEL PETRI
RE: CHRIS PAPPAS FOR SENATE
* **Independent polling continues to show Chris and former Senator John Sununu in a very competitive head-to-head matchup** with some surveys showing this race _within the margin of error._
* **National Republicans are now openly treating this as a top battleground race.** Senate leaders have rallied behind Sununu and touted New Hampshire as a key pickup opportunity.
* **Experts predict more than $100 million could be spent on this seat** with dark money already flooding in to distort Chris’s record and mislead Granite Staters.
* **GOP operatives are circulating memos arguing that with Sununu as the nominee, this seat moves from “competitive” to _flippable for Republicans._**

This all matters. _This race is not a foregone conclusion, and New Hampshire is not a safe blue seat._ New Hampshire voters are watching, GOP super PACs are throwing their support in, and the stakes could not be higher. Here’s what that means for our campaign:
Chris is running the strongest values-driven campaign possible: focusing on lowering costs for families, strengthening our economy, and defending individual rights. But when the other side is treating this race as a top pickup opportunity, we cannot afford to be complacent.
If there’s one thing you take away from this memo, let it be: **New Hampshire is highly competitive right now.** And this race will come down to turnout and organization. That’s why we must raise urgently needed resources to keep our campaign strong and in front of Granite Staters right now.
**To keep our efforts on track, we need to raise $150,000 before the end-of-year deadline.**
**[Contribute $5 now to join the fight to keep New Hampshire’s Senate seat blue.](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251228_3.N2Jhc2&refcode=em20251228-3&t=2&akid=4275%2E582294%2EbRR6T7)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251228_3.N2Jhc2&amount=5&refcode=em20251228-3&t=21&akid=4275%2E582294%2EbRR6T7)**
* * *
Thank you for standing with Chris and our team as we work to keep New Hampshire’s voice strong in Washington.
Rachel Petri
Campaign Manager, Team Pappas
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer?refcode=em20251228-3&t=22&refcode2=4275_582294_bRR6T7&akid=4275%2E582294%2EbRR6T7)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_update_your_information/?t=24&akid=4275%2E582294%2EbRR6T7)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=4275%2E582294%2EbRR6T7)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)

Manchester, NH 03105
United States

body:
We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. |
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141823/591700904/1412513779?amount=10&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.28_OTS-Mark-Kelly-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOWVhY2NlMS0zZWU0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=J3VVQLhr8RItjtttBaGR2wmf2siwdPjua8FPH-8WIOs=) Hey folks, it’s Mark Kelly. Seven years ago, I was making a decision about whether I was going to run for the Senate. I knew it would be a lot of work. A lot of time away from home. A lot of time away from family. I knew it wouldn’t be easy. But after two years of Donald Trump and a Republican-held Senate doing just about everything he wanted, I knew I couldn’t sit on the sidelines. I had to do something. So, I ran. And, somehow, we won. And then we had to turn around and run all over again to win a full term after that. But thanks to your support, we won that one, too. I’m grateful every day that I get to wake up, walk out the door, and defend our people, our democracy, and our Constitution. It’s one of the greatest honors of my life. And I’ve never forgotten that I wouldn’t be here without your help. We’ve been in some tough fights before, but this one is different. The President of the United States threatened my life, personally. I’ve got the entire executive branch bearing down on me right now. After everything you’ve already done for me, I shouldn’t have to ask you for more. But that’s what I need to do. **[I’m asking you to split a donation between Off the Sidelines and me, and I hope you know that I wouldn’t be asking if this wasn’t important.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141824/591700905/67485243?amount=10&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.28_OTS-Mark-Kelly-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOWVhY2NlMS0zZWU0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=J3VVQLhr8RItjtttBaGR2wmf2siwdPjua8FPH-8WIOs=)** _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Mark Kelly and Off the Sidelines PAC will go through immediately:_ | | [DONATE $10 NOW >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122141825/591700906/67485243?amount=10&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.28_OTS-Mark-Kelly-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOWVhY2NlMS0zZWU0LWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=J3VVQLhr8RItjtttBaGR2wmf2siwdPjua8FPH-8WIOs=)

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: This email is longer than usual
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
I want to tell you the action Powered by People took in 2025.
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
Think back to that first year of Trump’s presidency. Not long ago, right? The CEOs, the tech moguls, the media giants—they all fell in line. Law firms, Ivy League schools, Congress, even the Supreme Court—every institution with traditional clout seemed ready to bend to his will.
It was staggering, infuriating, and honestly, it could make anyone feel powerless.
_But we didn’t sit back or wait to be told what to do._
We chose to fight. To act. **Because action is the antidote to despair and it is the key to victory.**
This email is longer than usual, but I want to show you exactly how our Powered by People team took on 2025, rose to each challenge, and met each moment with strength and determination. It made a huge difference, and I'm proud of what we accomplished.
**It started with listening.** Showing up in every part of Texas to bring people together for no-holds-barred town hall meetings. We wanted to hear what was on people’s minds. Yes, their concerns but also their solutions to overcoming the very real challenges we face. People—Democrats, Republicans, Independents, non-voters alike—having conversations eyeball to eyeball. No question off limits, everyone allowed a chance on the mic.
We held town halls in Republican strongholds, places Trump had just won with 70% of the vote. Amarillo, Wichita Falls, Tyler. We had them in suburbs too: Mansfield, Rosenberg (with my friend Tim Walz), Denton, Garland, Humble. And we did the same in bigger cities, from San Antonio and Laredo to Beaumont.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=3&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Town hall in Garland_
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=4&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Town hall in San Antonio_
Everyone in the room learned from everyone in the room. In this age of misinformation and state-sponsored propaganda, it brought us together on the same page of truth, ready to take action. One moment that stood out was when a woman in Humble [talked about](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/65216?t=5&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b) what it means to have her health care taken away.
**Those community meetings at the start of the year led to unprecedented action in the streets.** People coming out in force for the Hands Off march in April and the No Kings protests after that.
Texas showed up, with events in the places you might expect but also in small towns and rural communities. Powered by People organizers were there every step of the way, registering voters, signing up volunteers, and getting folks added to our network to help them turn out in the next election.
We protested Trump’s cuts to foreign aid, his destruction of public education, his “Big Beautiful Bill” that takes the ability to see a doctor away from millions of people while funneling billions to his ultra-rich donors, his attacks on our democracy and our Constitution, the masked, plain-clothes officers he has sweeping our neighbors off the street without a badge or a warrant or any semblance of due process.
And we took it directly to him. When Trump invited himself to give the commencement at the University of Alabama in May, we went to Tuscaloosa and joined thousands of students and community members to offer a powerful, positive alternative to his selfish and destructive authoritarianism.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=6&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Together in Alabama_
But as spring turned to summer, it became clear to all of us at Powered by People that protesting wouldn’t be enough. We couldn’t just be against things. We needed to be for something.
**So we hit the road to share a big, bold, positive, unapologetic vision for our future.** One that tells people exactly how Democrats will deliver when we win power, incorporating what we had heard during our town halls earlier in the year.
We held rallies in Fort Worth (with our friend Bernie) and Amarillo to lay it all out. A future where you can afford to see a doctor, buy a home, send your kids to world-class public schools, and only have to work one job. A future where we have a real democracy; one where we no longer turn our backs on our friends and allies around the world.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=7&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_With Bernie in Fort Worth_
We took this Texas vision to the rest of the country, holding town halls in North Carolina, Pennsylvania, Virginia, Maryland, Wisconsin, Indiana, Missouri, Nebraska, Oklahoma, and Louisiana. We even held a memorable one in Berlin (Germany—not Texas) with Democrats Abroad to make sure that U.S. citizens living outside the country can use their power to vote to help win critical elections. Thousands of people came out to these events, allowing us to make them into big organizing opportunities... registering folks to vote and plugging them in to volunteer shifts in their local communities.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=8&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Town hall in Oklahoma City_
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=9&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Town hall in Charlotte_
While traveling to other states this summer, we also had the opportunity to build our coalition and our movement. We worked directly with partner organizations that share our priority of bringing people together and taking action. That meant joining the summer meetings of the Young Democrats of America, the NAACP, UnidosUS, and Netroots Nation.
All of this work—the travel, the organizing, the coalition building—was put to the test when Donald Trump ordered Greg Abbott and Texas Republicans to give him five more seats in the House of Representatives... a direct attack on Texas and an effort to seize political power before the first votes of 2026 were even cast.
But we weren’t caught flat-footed. In fact, we chose to aggressively fight the President and take our case to the country.
**I couldn’t be more proud of how Powered by People stepped up to lead this redistricting fight. As Republicans began to redraw the maps in order to steal five Democratic seats, we took action.**
We protested at the Texas Capitol and testified inside of it. We hosted a 6,000 person rally in Austin (with our friends Joaquin Castro, Jasmine Crockett, James Talarico, Gina Hinojosa, and Greg Casar). We held organizing events in Houston, Abilene, and El Paso. We brought together thousands more in Fort Worth, reminding Trump and the rest of the country that, while the rich and powerful might be genuflecting in anticipatory obedience to the little dictator, in Texas, **our knees don’t bend.**
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=10&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Rally in Austin_
During TV interviews, podcasts, and out-of-state events, we connected the dots for our fellow Americans about why what happens in Texas matters to them (Trump is trying to steal these seats in order to hang on to power in the House because if he loses his majority, there will finally be a check on his lawlessness, accountability for his crimes and corruption, and real hope for a better future for this country). We got off the back foot and on to the front foot, leading the call for Democratic-controlled states to fight fire with fire by redrawing their maps to cancel out the Texas gerrymander (thank you, California). We would no longer await the punch thrown by these would-be fascists. Instead we would throw ours first and throw it harder.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=11&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Rally in Fort Worth_
But we didn’t just raise awareness, we raised resources too. When the heroic Texas Democrats left the state to break quorum and stop Trump’s power grab, we had their backs. And we continued to have their backs when they returned. Thanks to our grassroots supporters, Powered by People donated over $1 million to the Texas Legislative Black Caucus, the Texas House Democratic Caucus, and the Mexican American Legislative Caucus over the course of the special session.
**Turns out Republicans don’t like when we fight back.** For standing against Trump’s Texas gerrymander, Ken Paxton accused me of bribery, tried to shut down Powered by People entirely, and even asked a judge to imprison me.
Did that stop us?
No. We continued to fight aggressively, taking Paxton to court, and winning in front of an all Abbott-appointed appeals court. I’m sure Paxton knew he could never prove his lies, but I’m also just as sure that his intent was to bury us in legal action and bankrupt us by turning the full power of the State of Texas against us. What he didn’t count on was that we were never, ever going to give in. Though the months-long fight cost us more than $400,000 in legal fees, our supporters stayed with us through the thick of it and helped us emerge stronger than ever.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=12&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=13&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
**So strong that we decided to do what’s never been done before—work with allies across Texas to fill every single seat on the ballot with a Democrat.**
We hit the road to recruit Democratic candidates in every part of the state. We hosted candidate recruitment events in breweries and bars, in church meeting rooms and community centers, on campuses... anywhere we could gather people to remind them what’s at stake and what we can do to meet the moment. And we weren’t just in the districts that are safe for Democrats. We were in Pearland, Waco, College Station, McKinney, and Fort Worth just to name a few.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=14&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Candidate recruitment event in San Antonio_
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=15&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Candidate recruitment event in Waco_
And here’s the result: Democrats filed to run for 39 out of 39 Congressional seats, 16 out of 16 State Senate seats, and 150 out of 150 State House seats. We’ve scoured the state records (which have been digitized back to 1974) and we can’t find another year where Democrats fielded a candidate for every seat.
But we know that it’s not enough just to have candidates on the ballot. They have to have our support too. That’s why Powered by People is organizing on the ground and on college campuses throughout the state. We are registering voters, building new voter relationships and signing up volunteers on campuses like Rice, University of Houston, UT, Austin Community College, UTSA, UTEP, and El Paso Community College. And we know this work works; the young voters who we met and registered and stayed in touch with in 2024 turned out at a rate of 79.5% (versus 42% nationally and 37% in Texas).
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=16&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
_Voter registration event at University of Houston_
**Finally, there was everything that our Powered by People volunteers were doing when I wasn’t physically with them.** When the media wasn’t there. When it wasn’t being documented for Instagram or TikTok. It was the voters being registered every single day. The volunteer shifts happening in every part of the state. The organizing for down ballot races like Senate District 9. And the texts sent, phone calls made, community events attended, action taken.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=17&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=18&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=19&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
We had a big year and we’re not slowing down now. We’re only going to turn it up in 2026. That's how we can win a U.S. Senate seat, critical Congressional races, state legislative seats... that's how we take back power, and use it to deliver for the people.
[**If you’re ready to build on this work in 2026, donate $3 today. Your donation will help us register voters, organize across Texas, and win races up and down the ballot.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&t=20&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)
**[Donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251228-2.qphd08&refcode=ema20251228-240567&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=39&akid=240567%2E444211%2EG_2f6b)**
Thanks for being in the fight with us.
Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
